text: I will start by explaining the basics of defence in Path of Exile, that will put everything in a greater context.
I will also provide you with an example budget build that utilizes the principles described in this guide, as well as a video guide and PoB for it.
_____________________________________ Part I: The main layers of defence.
_____________________________________ The main layers of defence can be separated into 4 different categories: 1) Avoidance - which is your character's ability to avoid damage - primarily from hits.

Examples of avoidance include: Chance to block attack damage, Chance to block spell damage, Evasion, Spell Dodge, Chance to avoid Physical damage from Hits, Chance to avoid elemental damage from Hits, Chance to avoid ailments, Chance to avoid all damage from hits (Elusive).
As you can see there are a lot of forms of avoidance, but almost all of them will protect you exclusively from hits and won't help you against degeneration / damage over time effects.
This is why it is important to incorporate multiple layers of defence into your character.
If your character naturally has a lot of avoidance, it's a good idea to look into adding a lot of recovery to it, as they synergize very well.
If your character isn't taking damage for a while but every now and then loses a lot of HP, you'll need an ability to quickly recover it, to avoid staying in a vulnerable position for too long.
2) Mitigation - is your character's ability to reduce the amount of damage taken.
This includes Elemental / Chaos Resistances, Armour, Physical Damage Reduction, Spell Suppression, Immortal Call, Lethe Shade, Fortify and more.
Mitigation is the layer of defence which will stop you from getting killed in 1 hit and will leverage your health pool to protect you from more damage.
3) Recovery - is your character's ability to gain back lost Health/Energy Shield.
This includes leech, gain on hit, regeneration, recovery, recuperation, recovery from flasks and other abilities.
Recovery is relatively easy to get while mapping but it is a much more scarce resource when bossing, however if you can solve that issue, your character will become a much better bosser.
Recovery is also great when you pair it with high avoidance, such as Life/ES gain on block or Ghost Shrouds.
4) Life Pool - is the total amount of hit points you have, which includes Life, Energy Shield and possibly Mana.

This is what people sometimes mistakenly call EHP (effective hit pool) - EHP is actually a much more complicated value which represents the combination of all your defensive layers against damage dealt to your character - you can check and compare this value in Path of Building, but it is not fully representative of characters survivability.

Your Life total is the most superficial layer of defence which is the first thing that people tend to notice, however you shouldn't be fooled by it because it is very easy to make a 4k hp character that will be twice as tanky as a 7k hp character.
As with all the other aspects of defence, this is just one layer of it.
That being said, the more you invest in avoidance, recovery and especially mitigation, the more value you'll get out of every single point of Life you add to your character.
These four main types of defence work best in combination.
It's the combination of many layers of defence that makes a character feel immortal.
Just like with damage scaling in PoE, the defence also goes up exponentially the more you put into it.
What makes a good character ______________________________________ Making a good character is not just about damage or defence, it is about efficiently ticking off all the right boxes.
Cast When Damage Taken Immortal Call pairs very well with Enduring Composure for example.
Granite flask with increased armour during flask effect can also offer a massive boost to your armour.

Many rare items can also offer modifiers such as "Additional Physical Damage Reduction" on body armour, or % of Physical Damage From Hits Taken As Fire/Cold/Lightning/Chaos - such as "20% of Physical Damage from Hits taken as Fire Damage" on the Cloak of Flame unique.
2) Elemental Damage - Elemental damage is very straightforward to deal with.
Unfortunately many people still have the attitude of - "my resists are capped at 75%, why did I die?".
Getting to 75% resistances is just a minimum requirement.
These days it is very easy to go above the 75% resist cap and you should definitely take advantage of that.
You can also take advantage of the "taken as" modifiers, such as "50% of Elemental Damage taken as Chaos Damage" on the Divine Flesh Keystone from the Glorious Vanity Timeless Jewel.
Increasing your maximum resistance is more powerful than most people realise because every additional point you get, gets you more and more damage mitigation.
To illustrate this in an easy example, let's say you have 0% fire resistance and you're going to take a 100 damage fire hit to the face.
If you get an additional 1% of fire resistance, you're going to now take 99 fire damage from that hit, which means you've reduced the incoming damage by 1%.
If you get that additional 1% of fire resistance, it will reduce the incoming damage from 11 to 10.
Which means at this point the same 1% of fire resistance is worth nine times as much in terms of reducing the damage you've been taking before.
This works similarly with mechanics like block, evasion and other hard % damage reductions (such as additional physical damage reduction).
3) Chaos Damage - Chaos resistance is one of the more neglected stats when it comes to easily available defences.
Softcore and Hardcore players alike are constantly dying to chaos damage while simultaneously refusing to get any serious amounts of it.
The game is not balanced around having 0% chaos res anymore, so if you're running with your chaos res value in the negatives, you're asking to get clapped by the Toxic Volatiles.
Good news is that if you decide to invest in your chaos res, you're going to feel significantly more tanky and the Toxic Volatiles will barely tickle you.

-You can find chaos res on the implicit modifier of certain bases such as Amethyst Rings or Fugitive Boots -You can force a chaos resistance roll with Essences of Envy -You can also use the bench to craft 15% elemental + 15% chaos resistance on rare items (this can be further increased by Prismatic Catalysts on Jewellery.

-There is chaos resistance available on many passive nodes on the passive skill tree, including the Asylum and One with Evil wheels which also give you access to very powerful passive masteries that are worth taking.
-If you have at least 38% positive chaos resistance, you might consider the Cleansed Thoughts anointment which doubles your Chaos Resistance.

-There is also an option of crafting a Small Chaos Resistance Cluster Jewel with up to 8% chaos resistance which together with the base 12% per node and a 35% increased effect of small passives can provide as much as 75% chaos resistance for 3 passive points + the jewel socket.
Even without going to that extreme, looking for chaos resistance on cluster jewels you're going to use anyway, is usually a good idea.
If you're looking for a good number of chaos res to aim for, I would recommend 40% positive chaos resistance + an amethyst flask which is going to take care of the remaining 35% as an easy solution.
You can stack evasion using the combination of your gear, passives, flasks and the Grace aura.
Evasion is also increased by your dexterity so if your character naturally gets a lot of it, it's probably a good idea to invest in some flat evasion to boost up your defences.
If you're using a significant amount of Evasion then it is also helpful to incorporate Blind as a defensive mechanic.
Blinded enemies will have a lower chance to hit through your evasion as well as give you a higher chance to hit them.
Attack Block is another way of avoiding damage from attacks.
You can increase your chance to block through modifiers on your shield and passive skills on the passive skill tree.

Glancing Blows keystone will also double your chance to block but I wouldn't recommend picking it up unless you also have some form or Life or Energy shield recovery on block that will justify taking the portion of blocked attack's damage.

**Overall block is an extremely strong defensive mechanic which will drastically increase your EHP if you invest in it.** It also has a lot of associated mods that can be found on unique and rare gear, ranging from increases to your block chance, to recovering a portion of your life whenever you block.
As before I recommend heading to https://www.craftofexile.com/ and typing "block" into the "search for an affix" window to find out more.

5) Spells - Damage from spells will be usually mitigated by your resistances or the physical damage reductions, but it is worth investing in another way of mitigating spells specifically - Spell Suppression.
It's a stat that can be found on the passive skill tree as well as modifiers on Evasion and Hybrid Evasion bases of boots, body armours, gloves, helmets and shields.
Spell suppression is especially effective against bosses, where you might encounter powerful single spell hits.
Getting your Spell Suppression to 100% chance, will mean that every single spell hit you take gets reduced in half (or by 55% if you also take the Inveterate + Spell Suppression Mastery).
If your character paths around the right/bottom part of the passive tree, it is highly recommended to utilise spell suppression.
Otherwise Acrobatics is not as good as relying on pure Spell Suppression.
Especially for characters which base their recovery on Leech/Life Gain On Hit, because you can not attack while stunned.
Stun is based around the damage done by a hit, compared to your maximum life.

Here are some ways of dealing with projectiles: -Soul of Lunaris pantheon upgrades, wich give you 10% chance to avoid projectiles and 100% chance to avoid chained projectiles (very important when playing with minions) -Flesh and Stone - less damage taken from enemies that aren't nearby -Arrow Dancing Keystone -Lioneye's Remorse but above all, one of the best defences against projectiles is to continuously move around the monsters which will make them miss most of their damage.
Most of the degeneration ground effects in the game currently come from two sources: Toxic Volatiles and Chilled Damage Over Time Ground.

To deal with the first one, you can: -Invest in chaos resistance as mentioned in one of the previous points -Use soul of Arakaali upgraded pantheon for 10% reduced DoT damage taken and 40% Chaos Resistance against DoT's.
Many people neglect life regeneration rolls on gear but they do add up and help your character deal with the constant pressure on your health put by the enemies.
The game always wants to kill you and it is never a bad idea to get a good amount of Life/ES regeneration.
That would require more than 150 mana reserved by Vitality.

If you're struggling with your mana reservation while using it, you can always pick the Life passive mastery for 100% increased mana reservation efficiency, allowing you to raise the level of your Vitality and get much more regen out of it.
Regeneration is one of the most powerful defensive mechanics in the game because it is unconditional and works regardless of how many enemies are around you and whether you can attack them or not.
10) Elemental Ailments [Freeze, Chill, Shock, Ignite, Brittle, Scorch, Sap] - Elemental Ailments are extremely dangerous and game changing, but also pretty easy to become completely immune to.
Degen will do more damage to you if you're shocked.
You will die at least ten times less frequently than before if you successfully protect your character from elemental ailments.

For most characters all you need to do is: -Use a life or mana flask with bleed removal/immunity -Take a mastery node on the passive skill tree for corrupted blood immunity or use a corrupted jewel with corrupted blood immunity implicit -You can also use Steel Skin to remove bleed -Soul of Ralakesh pantheon reduces the damage from bleeding drastically.
High amount of Chaos Resistance and sources of Chaos Resistance against DoT damage mentioned in the Degen section will take care of poison completely.

If your Chaos res is good enough, you will never need things like: -Poison remove/immunity on utility flasks -Unaffected by poison while affected by malevolence -Maw of Conquest Those extra poison protections are an overkill and only necessary for things like the Golden Rule Jewel builds.
Level 1–60: Offense is the Best Defense Early acts have no experience penalty on death , so you can go all-in on damage.
Acts 6–10: Build the Defensive Core From Act 6 onwards, resistances matter .
Chaos resistance is not mandatory yet, but some is recommended.
Entering maps without proper defenses is a recipe for frustration.
Level 70+: Building Real Survivability From here on, defense becomes as important as damage.
Chaos resistance : Aim for the cap, it becomes increasingly important in maps.
Spell Suppression : By now, you should already have around 50% chance to Suppress with ~50% damage reduction on spells.
Spells are the #1 killer in endgame, suppression is mandatory.
Ailment immunity : Freeze/Chill, Ignite, Shock → solved by Purity of Elements or specific gear.
Poison & Bleed → unique items or flask suffixes (auto-triggered).
Combine two or more: Armor + Evasion, Armor + Energy Shield, Evasion + Energy Shield.
Sustain mechanics : Life regeneration = slow, consistent recovery.
Life leech = strong for bosses, but delayed recovery.
Utility minions : Even a simple Golem can take aggro and reduce incoming damage.
Level 85–90: Refinement Stage Defense must be airtight, holes get punished hard here.
Damage must be high enough to delete packs quickly — dead monsters don’t deal damage (usually).
Automate flask immunities (bleed, curse, freeze if needed).
100% Spell Suppression is non-negotiable — no matter how good the rest of your defenses are, if you don’t suppress, spells will kill you.
Combine with capped resists, ailment immunity, chaos res, sustain layers, and flask automation.
Final Words Reaching level 100 is not about playing the meta, it’s about understanding the game’s mechanics and removing every hole in your defense while keeping strong offense.

Regards and have a nice time playing PoE ;) Last edited by Radwan1989#2271 on Sep 30, 2025, 10:51:19 PM Last bumped on Oct 2, 2025, 6:30:00 PM Posted by Radwan1989#2271 on Sep 30, 2025, 10:31:20 PM Quote this Post I have an easier one, for league start: until level 90-93, you only care about damage.
A 6L, 2k+ hp and capped resists should be enough to complete the atlas.
Once you have some currency (at least 5-10 divines, usually after you completed the atlas), decide if you start building defenses on that character, or start another.
Just use the crafting bench to add an extra mod on your gear - usually life, resists or a damage mod for your weapon.
Depending on the item (armour) and grouping of more desired mods (suffix/prefix), you can sometimes also use eldritch currency to cheaply reattempt for 5th/6th mods.

For items that have a desirable veiled mod (such as move speed on boots), starting with a fractured mod on the same side of the item as well (such as life on boots (both prefixes), makes using veiled chaos (vs exalts) a very cheap and viable option.
Roll suffix with essence (often chaos res essence until you hit a decent combo of chaos res + res+ res.
craft unwanted blocking mod (if chaos doesnt fill prefixes, which it usually doesnt) 6.
meta mods to preserve suffixes and cheap veiled chaos to reattempt.
Type of damage: First off, you need to know about existing types of damage in this game so as to know how to defend yourself.
Physical damage: done by most mobs, including self-detonating mobs, can be decreased by Armor or Endurance Charge.
Elemental damage: this includes Fire, Cold and Lightning.
You can tell if a type of mob is doing certain elemental damage by hover your cursor over it and see in the description.
Elemental damage can be reduced by Elemental Resistance up to 75% in normal condition.
You can improve that cap with Elemental Adaptation node and Resistance Flask .
Aside the main damage, these elemental damage can also caused status ailment.
Lightning can cause Shock (+40% damage for each stack, max = 3 stacks) 3.
Chaos damage: This type of damage bypasses all armor, energy shield and elemental resistance.
There are chaos resistance notes in the passive tree.
You can also increase your chaos resistance with equipment and flask.
Sometimes this type of damage is unavoidable (mobs with chaos damage over time aura) so you cannot kite it.

Other than stacking resistance there are 2 ways to mitigate it: have a lot of health and health regeneration or life leech and kill the mob before you are killed OR do an energy shield based build and take Chaos Inoculation passive node.
The effect of this node is making you immune to chaos damage but reducing your HP to 1.
So to survive against other types of damage while using Chaos Inoculation, you need a lot of Energy Shield, hence the ES + Chas Inoculation build.

The basis of all build: Survival The reason for why 'Survival' is the basis of all build is because: - You can't do damage if you die - Your damage output is 60% based on equipments, 30% based on sustainability and 10% based on passive notes.
- You are the enemy of yourself because there are mobs with reflect damage mod out there.
So unless you balance offense and defense, you are going to kill yourself.
So knowing that you are going to take all 3 types of damage mentioned above, you need to balance your build to mitigate all of them.
The more face to face you are going against mobs, the more points you need to invest into defense.
- Templar: This class is closest to strong shield blocking and resistance nodes and also next to Marauder and Witch areas.
Therefore you have 3 choices: + Hybrid: Scepter/Wand + Shield + good HP + armor (AR) based equipments + block + endurance charge + Elemental Adaption.
+ Strength: Scepter/Mace + Shield or 2-handed weapon + high HP + HP regeneration + maxed endurance charge + AR based equipment Elemental Adaption + Resolute Technique.
+ Intelligence: Scepter/Wand + Energy Shield (ES) based equipments + high ES + Chaos Inoculation.

Any ES based build is quite unfriendly to new players because you need to stack ES, which doesn't scale really well early game and you basically have 0 armor so it's like having a small pool of HP with 0 damage reduction.
(It is possible to build a bow Templar but you will have a hard time getting enough Dex to support your gem) - Witch: This class has the least amount of survival choice.
It only has access to weaker Life nodes (most are +8%), no life regeneration/life leech, lack of Strength so it's always recommended to do a ES + Chaos Inoculation build.
- Shadow: This class is even more recommended for ES + Chaos Inoculation build because aside from ES, it also has high Evasion Rating (ER).
Because Shadow is very far from both Blood Magic and Iron Reflex, and doesn't have access to high number of Strength node, it's difficult to do a life leech build.
However, don't count too much on evasion, it's like cream on top of a cake but not the whole cake.
Actually both Witch and Shadow can do ES build without Chaos Inoculation.
However you need to pick up at least 2 Chaos Resistance nodes and stack ES armors + Chaos Resistance nodes.
Best to balance all resistances to make this work.
Since you have a bigger 'life' pool than all other classes (HP + ES), less than maxed resistance is okay.
- Ranger and Duelist : The reason for why I list these two together because they are relatively equally close to significant Life nodes, Resistance nodes, Blood Magic , Iron Reflect and Vaal Pact .

Basically, all builds related to these 2 classes involve taking Iron Reflex as it grants them huge amount of AR, which could be greater than that of Templar/Marauder in full AR armor + shield, though the final damage reduction might not be as much due to not having access to Endurance Charge.
Aside from that, you can do either of these builds: + Life leech: in other words, Blood Magic + Vaal Pact + Life Leech support gem.
+ Non-LL: without life leech, I don't think melee can survive without endurance charge, high HP and HP regeneration.
He starts out with life, armor and resistance and also surrounded by endurance charge.

You can do many kinds of build with this guy except ER/ES based: + High HP + HP regeneration + block + AR + Elemental Adaptation + Endurance Charge + High HP + life leech (Blood Magic with/without Vaal Pact, if without, take HP regeneration) + Resolute Technique + Endurance Charge (if you want) + High HP + HP regeneration + AR + Elemental Adaptation + Dex + Int + Projectile Attack/Spell Any out of ordinary build will be heavily influenced by gears to make up for your shortcoming.
The reason why a HP + shield block build only needs good instead of high HP is because when you get the Templar's Shield Master group of nodes , as Templar or Marauder or Witch.
You will get +30% All elemental resistance, which let you invest more into chaos resistance in equipment.
Also your 'reflected' elemental damage will deal less to yourself.
A life leech build involves having enough HP for your skill casting as well as absorbing all types of damage while constantly leeching life from mobs to refill the HP pool.
Doing something like Infernal Blow + Blood Magic passive + Life Leech gem is not viable.

Offensive style: There are three main concepts that affect how you will improve your damage output: Note: any skill can be improved by increase critical strike multiplier so I won't mention this anywhere below.
Type of skill: Attack or Spell - Attack: any attack based skill will more or less involve physical damage with the exception of Elemental Hit.
Even the elemental damage portion will involve converting the base physical damage to elemental damage.
However, there are some attacks that do chaos damage which cannot be increased by any means.
Way to boost attack based skill, regardless of type of damage or characteristic of skill: Use Faster Attack gem.
- Spell: most spell does elemental damage with the exception of Ethereal Knives, Detonate Death, Shockwave Totem and Bear Trap.
Among those 4, only Etheral Knives's physical damage can be increased with passive nodes.
You cannot boost the damage of Bear Trap by any means.
Way to boost spell based skill, regardless of type of damage or characteristic of skill: Use Faster Cast gem and/or Iron Will (if you have high Strength).
Type of damage: - Physical damage: if it is an attack based skill then you need good weapon with high physical damage.
Therefore, most builds still rely on the elemental damage part.
- Elemental damage: if it is an attack based skill, you can use Weapon Elemental Damage gem or Weapon Elemental Damage passive nodes.
If it is a spell based skill, you can use weapon with Increase Spell Damage mod or Increaes Spell Damage passive nodes.
Increase Elemental Damage passive or mod will boost the elemental damage of both attack based and spell based skills.
Characteristic of skill: Melee or projectile, single target or AOE - Melee: Melee damage can be increased with Melee Physical Damage support gem.
- Projectile: Projectile damage can be increased with Faster Projectile, Lesser Multiple Projectile (LMP), Greater Multiple Projectile (GMP), Pierce, Fork, and Chain.
You need to use the appropriate support gem for each different projectile skill.
This may look great but each support gem (except Faster Projectile) will decrease the damage effectiveness of each projectile and increase the mana cost.
Most players choose LMP so that they can do a shotgun effect (hitting one mob with multiple projectiles) to increase damage.
So for the AOE part of these skills, you can improve their damage substantially with the Concentrated Effect gem by 1.5x at level 1, and even higher at higher level.

Some examples: - Lightning Strike: Lightning, Melee, Attack, Projectile: Melee Physical Damage, Faster Attack, Weapon Elemental Damage, LMP, Chain, Lightning Penetration, Increase Critical Damage, Life Leech.

- Fire Storm: Fire, Spell, AOE, duration: Concentrated Effect, Iron Will, Faster Casting, Increase Area of Effect, Increase Duration, Fire Penetration, Chance to Ignite, Elemental Proliferation, Increase Critical Damage, etc.
Choose which curse to use according to your type of attack and damage.
One more important thing to consider is whether your build involve enough base stats (Str/Int/Dex) for the skill/support gem that you want to use.
Sustainability: By sustainability, I mean mana cost.
The more gem you link, the more each use of skill costs you, mana or HP (if blood magic).
Also if you use aura, they will reserve a portion or amount of your total mana thus further decreases your mana pool.
- From passive nodes: Increase maximum mana by x%, Increase mana regeneration by x%, Reduce Mana cost by x%.
Although increase mana cost caused by support gem linked to aura/skill will increase their mana reservation, the reduced mana cost passive node will not decrease the mana reservation.
For that, you need the reduced mana reservation nodes, which only exist in the Templar and Marauder area.
- Reduced Mana support gem: This will greatly reduce the mana cost and mana reserve cost of any skill that is linked to it.
If you have 4L gloves for example, you can link Determination + Hatred + Purity + Reduced Mana to reduce the mana reserve of 3 Auras at the same time.
- Use totem: you can use Spell Totem support gem for Spells or Ranged Attack Totem for projectile attack skills.
Basically, instead of using your skills directly, you will summon a totem which continuously use these skills for you at 0 mana cost, however at reduced speed and weaker damage.
Other modification support gem can be linked to alter the skill effect used by totem too.
(Aiming for a particular playstyle) - Look, this pair of gloves makes unarmed damage actually hurt.
- That's an interesting damage formula...let me see exactly how many "more" multipliers I can get to work together at once.
Skills and supports If the starting point was a skill, that's the primary skill.
Generally, the 'hook' will specify implicitly some criteria that the active skills have to fulfill: must be AoE, should be usable while unarmed, etc.
Sometimes only a combination of skills will fulfill the criteria: melee splash and multiple projectiles are notable for this reason.
Naturally, one must then pick support skills to go with the active skills.
Support gems in items (looking at you, Bringer of Rain) do not count toward this limit.
I have a list of support gems in hard copy format just so I can go down the list and pick out any that might work.
These are generally the ones that require few supports.
Some requirements are universal, such as HP/ES (as appropriate) and resists, but some are not.
Besides the obvious damage roll, cast speed and local crit are probably the biggest offenders here.
This also applies to armour and accessories, but weapon is the most important one.
I have no numbers for hybrid builds except "as much as possible", because hybrid HP/ES builds don't really have the space to get efficient mitigation from other sources like armour.
- Crit builds should aim for every relevant and available (and efficient) crit node; multiplier is less important as this is more effectively gotten from equipment.
If you can't get >300% crit from passives, you're doing it wrong.
Odds are you'll need the stats for your support/utility gems or auras.
For example, there's critical chance clusters offering 100% and 105% increased global critical chance; however the 105% cluster takes 5 points, while the 100% cluster only takes 4 points.

At first glance the 105% increased global critical chances sounds more, but at the cost of 1 extra skill point; you're basically gaining 21% increased critical chance per point, while the 100% cluster gives 25% increased critical chance per point, while also giving 5% critical strike multiplier.
You don't have to always take the full cluster, the 105% cluster can give you 60% critical chance just for 2 points!

While on the another hand, the 100% cluster can give 60% critical chance and 20% critical multiplier for just 2 points too, often you won't be right next to it, unless you already grabbed coordination or spell power.
For example, if you need two points just to get to the 105% cluster, that makes your critical strike chance effectively 15% per point; that's something to think about.

The fourth stage of skill point effectiveness involves the use of equips; for example 20/20 increased critical damage multiplier gem gives 149%; while 20/20 increased critical strike gem only gives 125%.

A skill point for the common critical multiplier is 10%, while for critical strike is 15%: this makes it a lot better to just invest into critical chance nodes and use increased critical damage gem, rather than invest into critical damage nodes and use increased critical chance gem.
You can stack a lot of critical chance, up to 95%, but if your accuracy is shit (for attackers), most of the critical roll won't do anything.
In the end you will have shit damage (because focusing so much on critical) performance.

On the other hand, even if you're able to stack critical chance to 95%, accuracy also 90% or higher; you will still do only average damage; because you sacrificed so much +% damage nodes that your 250%+ critical damage is about the same as people who just stack +% damage nodes, and -1 support gem on your main skill.

I personally believe a spec really unfolds at lvl 90+ so, if you're not seeing noticeable gain in efficient points at that point, you should possibly think you maybe should chose a path that offers a bit less good setup at lvl 70 but will scale way greater at lvl 90, remember at that point you will probably have access to better equipment, that will support that even more.
The first issue you run into will probably be resource management.

Up to about merciless, it's generally expected that you can/will spam mana flasks while using 3-linked skills; finishing out the storyline and reaching the endgame, it becomes possible to reduce or completely remove your flask dependence where mana is concerned.
There are four distinct ways to resolve mana issues without resorting to flasks: Blood Magic, mana leech, Eldritch Battery, mana regen.
Several unique items also offer alternative solutions, and some claws also have mana on hit.
The solution you finally settle on may be one or a combination of the above, or you may choose to just keep the mana flasks, especially if you're using Surgeon's flasks on a crit build.
Rules of thumb: if there are DPS problems, first look to get a level-appropriate weapon if applicable, then look to your skill links.
Multiple ones, actually.) If there are survivability problems, pick up HP/ES/resists on gear as appropriate, and try to find some way of running endurance charges if you don't already use them.
And if you have the currency to spend, uniques like Lightning Coil provide great damage mitigation, which is why they're so expensive.
Actually, let's talk more in-depth about defenses.
With the advent of 2.0, layered defenses have now become necessary rather than nice.
Even with a 9k EHP pool, if incoming damage is not mitigated in some way (and preferably multiple ways), you are likely to go splat if something so much as brushes you on its way past.
If I then add Mind over Matter to the mix, I take 30% of damage to mana first - so I can take approximately 14k damage before I die, hence 14k EHP.
As large as your EHP pool is, at some point reducing incoming damage will have better returns.
We'll start with the easy one: direct reduction from armour, which stacks additively with reduction from chaos golem, Fortify and endurance charges.
For this reason, most people bring around at least one Granite Flask (or Jade if they also took Iron Reflexes).
Uniques such as Lightning Coil and Taste of Hate are very expensive in HC leagues, and for good reason: they convert part of physical incoming damage to elemental.
This has two effects: one, it reduces incoming physical damage, making armour mitigation more effective due to the way the formula works.
Two, the portion converted to elemental is further reduced by elemental resistance.
The end result is that a character running LC might be taking maybe half the damage of an identical character without LC.
Or you could approach it from the other side and run Enfeeble to reduce the damage mobs deal, right at the start.
And what if we could simply not take hits at all (damage avoidance)?
Evasion in this game is deterministic, which means that if you have a 20% chance to be hit, you'll take damage every 5th hit, no questions asked.
(The first few are random until you get hit once or the counter reaches 4 so the next one becomes autohit.) And as a note, you can apply Blind to enemies in some way, to reduce their accuracy.
Dodge is not evasion, they're different things in-game.
Dodge is true random, so if a hit gets through your evasion counter, you roll for dodge.
If a hit gets through your evasion counter and you fail to dodge it, you roll for block.
Between evasion, dodge and block, you can reasonably set up to take maybe 20% of incoming damage - but that 20% is going to hit you full force unless you also have some mitigation in place.
I once made a full ev/dodge/block character who one-shot herself every time she met physical reflect and managed to hit herself.
I should mention, at this point, that Enfeeble also reduces mob accuracy.
Arctic Armour, Temporal Chains, Decoy Totem, et cetera.
blood magic for CI), and reasonable damage/utility contribution and survivability in higher maps according to your preferred playstyle (solo or group).
I will concede, however, that SC players can afford to skimp on defenses while leveling up, where HC players generally cannot.

You also want your Resistances to be capped at all times; a total of 135% to all elemental resists by the time you Map is probably the bare minimum you want in combined stats (i.e.
If you are an Attack/Weapon build, the vast majority of your damage is likely to come from your Weapon, so you usually have to shell out a certain amount of cash to get a good weapon.
In that case, there should be a ton of Unique items with ridiculous DPS for their cost (i.e.
You'll probably hit Maps with your Spells around level 16, which is workable, but each level grants about 8% more damage, compounding, so you'll get noticeably more oomph as you level up.
For both Attack and Spell builds, most of the time, getting more damage Supports in your main damage link-up scales your DPS literally exponentially.
This is because "x% More Damage" always stacks multiplicatively, and most damage-based Support gems nowadays include some amount of x% more Damage.
It's not quite (1.69)^5, as there aren't enough high-damage Supports, but you get the idea.
For a conservative example, suppose you already have Lacerate - Melee Physical Damage - Added Fire Damage - Faster Attacks.
Then, you add on Multistrike and Hypothermia.* Multistrike level 16 has 90% more attack speed and 30% less attack damage, so it's a net 1.9 * 0.7 = 1.33 or 33% more DPS.
Hypothermia level 16 has 35% more damage, or 1.35 multiplier.
Together combined, 1.33*1.35 = 1.7955, or nearly 80% more damage.
If you level these gems to 20, it becomes 1.94*0.7*1.39, or 1.8876, or nearly 90% more damage.
That's 10% "more damage" short of doubling your damage.

*Hypothermia is chosen here because Chilled Ground, such as from Arctic Armour or Cast-when-Damage-Taken-level-1-Arctic-Breath can reliably Chill practically any enemy for a new player, including bosses, as only certain special "advanced" bosses are immune to Chill.

IGN: Eric_Lindros CET: Timezone Posted by Ludvator#6587 on Mar 7, 2017, 5:04:24 PM Quote this Post I think the biggest bait to the guides that scream "### DPS!!!" and such, is that they "neglect" to mention that they need ridiculously min-max gear.
Most people think that the second they get the gear, their damage reaches the breaking point.
Proper links, skill gem level (especially for spells), quality, and all that, play a huge role in your damage output.
My damage nearly doubled when I swapped over from the 5L Brain Rattler (~480 weapon DPS ) to the 6L Atziri's Disfavour (~600 weapon DPS ).
Elemental Hit) or support gems (Greater Multiple Projectiles [GMP], Chain) that can only be found by looting or trading with others.
If you need specific support/skillgems, consider rolling another toon that will receive that skill/support gem.
Defense over Offense - Try to maintain a greater ratio of defense to offense.
Do not spec all into offense or throw a passive point or two into defense.
Likewise, do not spec all into defense as it will you get you nowhere either!

- The three defense aspects you have to consider are: 1) primary defense (life or ES) 2) secondary defense type (armor, evasion, acrobatics), 3) resistances - Generally speaking, having 2k+ life, 40%+ armor or evasion, and 20+ resistances will be enough when starting Merciless difficulty, but these are the bare minimum figures and should strive for more beyond that.
By the time you hit endgame at lvl 66+, you'll want 3k+ life, 50%+ armor/evasion, and 50+ resistances.
- Watch out for mobs dealing Chaos damage, shock stacking/freeze, and critical damage.
What do you want to accomplish with this character with the available skill/support gems?
Think about the synergy of skills with support gems and support/utility skills.
Physical, Elemental, single element, crit, or a mix?
Successful builds in this game usually require a combination of 2 types of damage.
Armor through Iron Reflexes, or evasion + Acrobatics/PhaseAcrobatics + Arrow Dodging?
Mana Flows, leech, regen) or Blood Magic (keystone/support, HP, HP regen)?
Watch your Flask selection - Certain flasks (resists, diamond, granite) are drop only, and cannot be purchased from the NPC vendor.
- Diamond flasks are now being reinstated into the game in a nerfed manner.
Crit builds can now utilize this flask as a way to increase your chance of criticaling.
- Quicksilver flasks will replace the overrused Phase Run.
Think about whether or not you want this flask to use up one of your slots on your flask belt.
^^ Chances are they wont be supporters, so they can learn from a little bit of use and abuse like most of us probably did.

Posted by havenrl#3780 on Jan 21, 2013, 12:06:12 AM Quote this Post A few notes I found very useful Granites can roll a % Increase to Evasion, meaning that acrobatics users and/or evasion users can put a granite to good use, even if the armor is useless.
For example rolling a high physical damage mod on a magic item can go a long way when you start with nothing.
Life, life, and more life (or if CI, which I don't recommend when starting out, ES) I cannot stress how important life is in this game due to the increase in mob damage.
Also all builds utilize Iron Reflexes (IR) to convert evasion to armor.
Having armor makes it easier to manage your secondary defense than having to dump lots of passives into evasion/Acrobatics+PhaseAcrobatics/ArrowDodging.

(Also more reliable defense.) One last thing to note is that most Ranger builds are based around elemental damage - that's unfortunately how the game is currently set up with ele mods being more numerous and easier to build up than physical damage.
Elemental damage stacks nicely with Frenzy, and like Build 1, this build uses Anger/Wrath/Grace with LA for AoE and Frenzy for single-target.
You can use flat-mana auras by using the Blood Magic support gem on top of stacking 3 %-based auras on your mana pool.
This increases your defense AND offense but sacrifices some slots that could be used for utility skills.
While it has less health that the previous elemental builds, it offers Critical damage that can easily match its own AND exceed DPS of that of elemental builds.
Any auras can be used, but consider using 2 %-based auras, and 2 flat-cost auras on BM support gem (if you have enough life.) Mana Leech on gear is a must to power this build.
Hatred aura is great for this.) Visit my own version of this build using Evasion/Dodge here !

It used to be that RAT gem got 50% less damage at level 1 and made it instantly a bad choice with the penalties to 30% less attack speed as well.
In 2.5, it got the same treatment as the Spell Totem support gem did in 2.4, so that at higher levels, the damage penalty was 35% at level 1 (the old lvl 20 RAT dmg penalty) and 26% at lvl 20.
This opened the door to create RAT builds that were more than just a super hipster low damage meme build.

I have played both close to 'meta' totem builds in the past -- Crit Flame Totem, Searing Bond (when it was OpieOp) , Shockwave Totems, Warchief Totems, Flameblast Totem both PRE-Totem AI fix and after.
I tried making it work by using Warped Timepiece's less duration with less duration support gem getting the duration to 2.8 seconds.

Despite Frost Wall no longer working/interacting with Kinetic Blast -- which is highly unfortunate because this build would be an *amazing* boss killer with that still working as it used to -- I still wanted to use the insanely low opportunity cost for great consistent damage by using the Inquisitor templar.

Thematically, Templar is my favorite class as I generally gravitate to STR/INT hybrid classes in most RPGs -- but the inquisitor just feels so consistently strong with its ability to ignore mob ele resists on crit.
While Ele Crit builds are completely viable -- and in some cases stronger -- with other classes, Inquisitor is just a total badass.
Combine all of these things, and the new unique in 2.6 -- Lycosidae shield giving a global'Hits can't be evaded' means you have 100% accuracy on a wander build.
If you've never played a wander build or a build utilizing a Vagan modded dagger, you may not understand how STRONG 100% accuracy is for crit builds.

For those needing an explanation for why it's so strong (100% accuracy) I have created a section "Why Lycosidae" Pros/Cons Spoiler Pros-- -Inquisitor is a purely "Ethical" build , no double dipping/poison 'exploits'.(I have experimented with variants involving poison, but that is not covered here) -Awesome farming build and a cheaper, easier alternative to dried lake vaal spark farming.
-relatively very cheap initial investment - 10%+ ES regen and 4%+ mana regen upon using Sulphur Flask with just one actual passive tree node with regen.

-can do atziri ezpz -one of my friends that I map with, "You fixed the whole wander movement speed problem by not having to actually stop and cast Kinetic Blast" -confuse people and have them ask you what actual build you are playing -Memeworthy clearspeed for a build far from what would be considered 'meta' Cons -No Whirling blades/Shield Charge :( -Movement speed with flasks down FeelsBadMan -Peddling flasks pretty regularly to keep damage consistent.

This is not a con for those not used to it, but if you're a new player, this is a good build to learn flask management -If you can't play a CI build with less than 10k es, may want to skip, although you could build this without the hits cant be evaded shield....just going to get super pricey with all the rolls you would need on jewelry.
I prefer self-cast wander but this is more of a lazy man's build without the true need for life/mana leech to keep attacking.
- Without further investment into totem life/resist, your totems (even lvl 20 RAT) can die easily in some situations making some boss fights a pain.

No uniques are *100% required* but there are few that are recommended to boost quality of life significantly Spoiler I would say the most important uniques, or ones you should prioritize: Crown of Eyes -- Staple of any wand build, and adds a ridiculous amount of damage.
Makes it so anything with "Spell Damage" actually mean "Attack Damage".
"Your hits cannot be evaded" eliminates the need for accuracy rolls on gear, accuracy nodes on the tree, and makes any wand you equip essentially a vagan-modded weapon.
Having 100% accuracy makes the build feel so much better while leveling or farming and makes single target a bit less shit.
Dying Sun-- It goes without saying, but this is just a huge quality of life improvement to give more projectiles essentially for free, at the cost of a flask slot.
Voidwalker-- I'm adding this here because it's a great way to get 100% pierce chance on your kinetic blast with the help of the quartz flask (which gives you phasing).
This frees you to take a different gem slot (like inc crit strikes or even inc crit dmg gems) without the need for the Pierce gem, which only gives 50% pierce chance.
"You have Phasing if Energy Shield Recharge has started Recently" , along with 10% movespeed, 6% dodge while phasing , 100 flat es, 10-15% all res and 300 flat evasion.
You can also run Brinerot Whalers, as they have some nice damage boost + resist with a respectable level of ES.

/ Accuracy and Crit explanation Spoiler Without going into too many mechanical details for those that don't know, I'll give you the TL;DR for why this shield is highly recommended, closest thing to a requirement without a high budget where you can afford high Flat accuracy, Flat ele damage, WED, and resists on rings/ammy.
For example, a well rolled ele attack ring with flat accuracy could easily run you 1-2ex at the very bare minimum.
This is why this shield will carry your damage at a much lower cost!
So, explaining the mechanics of why it's so good and why stacking accuracy gets expensive and can hamper your damage otherwise.
Accuracy for if you hit a mob AND the success of if you will crit or not *IF* the hit lands rolls separately for each attack.
You have 50% critical hit chance, and you have 85% chance to hit.
You have 85% chance to hit the monster You 36.125% chance to land a critical strike (85% * 85% * 50%) Let's say that you increased your chance to hit to 90%, with the same 50% crit chance.

You have a 90% chance to hit You have 40.5% chance to land a critical strike (.9 * .9 * .5) This means that for attack based builds, stacking an obscene amount of critical strike chance becomes less meaningful if you don't have very high accuracy.
Most crit builds that I have seen have around an 80-85% chance to hit at end game.
Having low accuracy severely handicaps your ability to land a critical strike.
In the case of this build, if you are not critting to get the full benefit of Inevitable Judgement, you are missing out on a large portion of your damage!
So with the shield : You have a 100% chance to hit You thus have a 45% chance to land a critical strike BECAUSE you have a crit chance of 45%.
With a diamond flask ("Critical hit chance is lucky") it will roll your crit chance twice on each hit making it much more likely that each hit crits.
You can see this being played on Havoc's stream http://www.twitch.tv/havoc616 He plans on playing with necro support, and has a buttload of movement speed.
You could run Hierophant with the free + 2 totems at the downside of a bit less damage.
Would allow you to run a Mantra of Flames jewel for actually great burst damage and the potential for free power charge generation (when placing a totem).
The Sanctuary of Thought notable also is an option , as that will give you free totem cost -- when the totem normally cost 80+ mana a piece, that's a pretty big benefit.
Occultist works fairly well with either Ele or Phys/Chaos scaling and requires a few tweaks.
To be clear, on-kill effects will work if the target is killed by a Damage over time effect (DOT), so Southbound basically forces it to recognize each kill as your own.
If your charges fall off, it's going to be struggle city, but with them up, the build has a feeling that the only thing limiting your DPS is the attacks per second.
Stupidly strong and will accelerate your damage considerably once you pick it up.
Since we can't leech, life regen via Zealot's Oath is a great way to mitigate a lot of lower-level damage.
With Pious Path regen which stacks with consecrated ground generated via Sulphur Flask, we get 10% regen and 4% mana regen/sec from consecrated ground!
A huge boost, but we also get an additional 50% damage and 25% cast/attack speed from consecrated ground as well, making it super strong for what we want to do.
Charge Generation Spoiler You can use Orb of Storms with Power charge on crit (PCOC) and a few other options like a curse on hit setup.

What I did want to do to get a feeling that I was at least contributing myself since this is a totem build, I have been using a 4L -- Frenzy - GMP - PCOC - Knockback *OR* Inc Crit Strikes which does a great job at generating charges , but I will emphasize that charges are not a requirement for this build as you can get by and farm without generating any.
I mainly used it for burst and single target damage for bosses.
Gem Links Spoiler Ranged Attack Totem- Kinetic Blast - WED - Pierce - optional 5 and 6L gems: Added Lightning, Added Cold, Faster Attacks, Minion and Totem Elemental Resists.
**IF YOU USE VOIDWALKERS, SWAP PIERCE FOR SLOWER PROJECTILES** It's essentially a 10% more damage boost, at the cost of not having pierce before you kill something.
You will find that the Minion and Totem Elemental Resistance gem (recently?) had 10-19% MORE elemental damage added in addition to making the totems more tanky.
Ideally Piscators would be best in something like a low life version, but I don't know how to make a low life build feel good with both damage and survivability.
He uses voidwalkers and quartz flask to bypass the need for the Pierce support gem.
With the hits can't be evaded shield and Inevitable judgment + Diamond flask, things can start to feel real nice!

Unlike conventional Revenant Reaper, this build uses Fresh Meat and Unleash support, and requires constant resummon Reaper every 10 secs, the playstyle is more SRS summoner like.
And it got buffed in 3.28, becomes 20% more minion damage (from 15%), makes me wanted to play with it even more.

While this helmet provides huge bonuses to minion through Whisper , it has been very difficult to make use of it, because of the chaos damage over time downside to the minion, causing your minions die very quickly.

With Prolonged Service, it not only prevents your minions dying from the chaos dot, the chaos dot also makes your minion goes into low life quickly, and get the 20% more minion damage from Prolonged Service.

(Whispers: Minions which hear the whispers take 20% of their maximum life as chaos damage per second, have 50% increased attack speed and damage, and do not listen to you) Prolonged Service also allows the build to go all in investing into minion offensive power without taking care of their survivability, which is a huge upside.

Ancient Skull helmet also requires minion critically hit to gain the buff, which makes Fresh Meat a great support, it provides massive critical bonus and Adrenaline buff to the minions when they are newly summoned.
Fresh Meat Support's buffs apply to minions based on 10% of their minion duration, up to a maximum of 10 seconds.
To make full use of Fresh Meat support, we need a permanent minion that is easy to summon, and with no cooldowns.
Because we need to resummon 5 Reaper every time Reaper dies, Unleash support becomes handy.

This nodes will kill any target affected by offering when the duration expired, including player, making the build cannot ultilize bone offering to gain block chance, so I go with Gladiator for easy max block and lucky block.
Build Mechanic Spoiler Reaper Consume with Ancient Skull Spoiler Consume (Heal): The Reaper will use this ability if it has less than 75% life, there is an allied damageable minion nearby.
The Consume skills kill one allied damageable minion, then recovers 50% of the Reaper's life and applies a buff to the Reaper for a base duration of 6 seconds.

40% increased attack speed 40% increased movement speed 30% increased Area of Effect 10% increased character size Ancient Skull helmet chaos dot will reduce Reaper's life very quickly and hence triggering the consume to buff Reaper offensively.

(9) Unveil the modifier by right clicking item and select Hybrid Increased Physical Damage + Bleeding/Poison/ Impale/ Blind (this has a 95% chance to appear, but to make it 100% you can even craft elemental penetration from crafting bench before unveiling).

Tattos can be movement speed, projectile speed, spell suppression or accuracy if needed.

(remember proj speed is damage because of your bow mastery) ---Elemental hit vs lightning arrow vs Tornado shot Ele hit is by far the best option at low budget because it's the only bow skill that provides a massive amount of flat damage, which is a stat you lack on low budget/league start.
---Pantheon: soul of the brine king until you're ailment immune(100% chance to avoid elemental ailments or purity of elements), after you can swap to solaris or lunaris.
You only play warden when you need very high single target damage, usually this scenario is doing t17/nightmare maps, as the bosses can be extremely tanky.
And eventually When you can get a ff/ff, than warden loses a lot of value, since you can get oath of spring or avatar of the wilds and have massive single target damage on deadeye.
Of course you could drop point blank if you already have enough damage.
---Trinity Support A common misconception with trinity is needing to deal equal dmg with 3 elements.
---Fledging vs no fledging and optimal single target damage.
- Yes far shot from the helmet and ascendancy do stack together, giving you 90% more damage.
Fledging is by far the best choice for pure map blasting, as it's just often 60% more damage at 70m, but it's a downgrade on bossing and something you swap off of if doing boss content early on.

For pure boss damage, it's most optimal to be in point blank range, to constantly get a double hit with your LA/ele hit - You wont get a double hit if you're far shot range, because tornado spawns near you.

This means if you're doing boss content, it's best to click point blank on your skill tree and stand in melee range vs bosses and it would be best to drop return proj for another damage support (inspiration) Keep in mind, this does drastically change when you acquire Nimi's, as the ring make fledging optimal for both bossing and mapping, as you're able to stand in melee range and still get max far shot value.
---Hyrri's Ire vs Daresso's vs Rare chest On ele hit daresso's is roughly equal DPS to hyrri's, but doesn't provide 30% spell suppression.
20% MS is also very nice early on with daresso's Hyrri's ire is the highest damage option, but can be expensive on league start.
Allows you to match the dps of hyrri's, while providing resist or attributes and giving equivalant evasion - Don't go for this option till you can make an all T1-T2 chest.
Yoke is a pretty late game option, the biggest reason to swap to yoke is to help with resist.
Grace - Haste - Anger - Purity of elements Anger is only really used when you can use 3 50% auras, as the damage it provides isn't massive while playing elemental hit.
This depends if you want more speed vs higher evasion for defense.
Purity of elements just helps with resist and gives cheap ailment immunity, later on when you can afford an Ancestral vision, you'll swap off of Purity of elements.
You can also drop purity of elements after getting tamings, since they give a large amount of resist.
Keep in mind when you swap off of purity of elements, you will want to achieve 100 ailment avoid.

When entering Maps I prefer to use haste and anger and swapout one of them for grace in red maps (t11+) ---Lethal pride You're looking for a 2 double damage or 3 DD lethal pride - My video on how to search for them efficiently - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_kYy-s_B-ng ---How do you proc bleed for cornered pray?
The small nodes on this wheel already gives you % chance to bleed and dealing any amount of phys damage can bleed, so even lightning arrow/torando shot will bleed enemies.
For Elemental hit, you'll use a manaforged arrow setup with puncture, to gaurentee a bleed on bosses.
Giving you up to 90% more damage at max range, which is 70 units.
It just provides a ton of increase damage and resist, making it one of the best rings you can use until mirror rings.
---Good jewel stats For elemental hit, any crit multi works because the skill is all elements.

The skill converts the defensive stats on your shield into flat damage.
Our EHP goes over 550K and our Combined DPS reaches ~30 million (assuming a 2 Overlapping Waves hit).
The main shifts in the approach in this variant are: 1 --> Armour Stacking, we now use Untiring with a Replica Dreamfeather as our 1-H, which adds a global damage modifier based on our armour.
2 --> 90 Max all Resist achieved via a Flesh/Flame combo for Valako, Storm's Embrace .
This does not really express itself in the POB, but you will see its uptime and value in Delve for sure.
Look for an implicit on these that gives at least +0.7% to Critical Hit Chance (this is a base crit modifier, not just additive).
To explain a few: 1 --> Replica Dreamfeather replaces our longstanding unique sword to capitalize on the % Attack Damage per 450 armour.
3 --> Our Flesh/Flame combo jewel activates Valako, Storm's Embrace , allowing us to Cap all resistance with ease.

Search the mods related to Precision , Determination , and/or Pride with this link here: https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Watcher%27s_Eye 5 --> The Light of Meaning (Armour) further stacks our armour, which has various synergies, including a direct damage scaling with Dreamfeather.
Do note that a nice boofy Defiance of Destiny is ultimately what holds up the Cold and Lightning Resistances to the 90 cap.
Make sure your defiance has at least 20% of the recovery effect.

Top choices to consider: 1 --> Sanctuary --> Defensive node that gives you a +1 max if needed (must hit 90 all) 2 --> True Strike --> Massive DPS / crit node 3 --> Forceful Skewering --> Can cap off Impale chance to 100 if you aren't there yet Also note that the Ruby Flask is not a meme, we simply want to have permanent flask enchantment of 3% of life per second.
Now that Enlighten Support is assumedly affordable, we can slot in the best in slot reservation gems around the board.

Changelog Carrion Golems & Zombies Necromancer League Starter for Path of Exile Mirage 3.28 by Helm Breaker Mirage 3.28 Last Updated: July 1, 2026 Follow Changelog FAQ Get Link Advertisement This website is supported by ads.
This build uses Convocation to stack minions, improving clear and allowing you to focus all your damage on a single target.
The build uses Carrion Golems and Zombies to deal high Poison and Chaos damage.

For defenses you rely on a high Life pool, Life regen, Life leech, Endurance charges, Physical damage reduction, Energy Shield , increasing Maximum Elemental resistances and high attack and spell block chance.
Later on you need to get a Ashes of the Stars , Amanamu's Gaze , Primordial Might , Primordial Harmony , The Anima Stone and Rational Doctrine to min-max your endgame damage.

Carrion Golems & Zombies Necromancer League Starter Gear Set Examples Resistances and Attributes You do not need specific resistances and attributes on any item, just get the required total resistances and attributes amongst all gear combined.
You need to cap your Resistances at 75% unless otherwise specified in the guide!
Note : Having extra elemental resistances over your max cap helps dealing with elemental exposure and weakness.
Buy Holy Flame Totem and Flame Wall from Nessa for two extra damage skills.
You can get Chance to Poison Support by muling it from a Shadow alt after killing Hillock or You can get the gem from the optional "Mercy Mission" quest in Act 1 as a Witch.
Your Raise Zombies can easily die before you reach level 21 and allocate the Minion Defence Mastery " Minions recover 5% of life on Minion death ".
Make sure not to level Clarity and Vitality too many times because of the increased mana reservation.
(If you level up or de-level the gem, go back to Act1 and buy a new one) After completing the quest "Lost in Love" in Act 3 take Conductivity to curse rares and bosses for increased damage.
You can get many of the gems you need such as Added Chaos Damage Support around level 31 by completeing Siosa's quest in The Library (Act3).
In Act 4 use Summon Stone Golem for the Life regeneration buff.
Around level 46 when you have both Sovereignty and the Mana Mastery " 12% increased Mana Reservation Efficiency of Skills ", equip and activate Purity of Elements .
After the Campaign, you can continue to use Absolution or Summon Raging Spirit in white and yellow maps until you have the correct support and skill gems.
Replace Summon Stone Golem and Faster Attacks Support with Steelskin linked with Automation .
You need to get Void Manipulation Support , Multistrike Support and Melee Splash Support for your Golems and Zombies.
For mapping you use Melee Splash Support and then swap it out for Multistrike Support when you fight bosses.
When you use a trigger wand for Flesh Offering and Desecrate make sure you keep Desecrate at level 1 to reduce the mana cost.
Note : If your AG has Asenath's Gentle Touch or Cane of Kulemak and Gravebind you no longer need to use Melee Splash Support .
Use a Perfect Guardian Turtle for the 5% flat physical damage reduction buff and a weaker Determination aura.
You can use an Arena Master for more damage until you acquire the right Spectre corpse.
Bone Offering massively improves the survivability of your Minions and it provides you with a lot of block chance if you have the Mistress of Sacrifice Ascendancy node.
Note : Respec Commander of Darkness to Mistress of Sacrifice when you have enough elemental resistances (105%) to be overcapped without it.
Replace Absolution with Stormblast Mine linked with Minefield Support .
This skill is only used to debuff tanky rares and bosses so that they take 3% increased damage per nearby mine.
When your Minion's Poison chance is 100% without Chance to Poison Support , replace it with Ruthless Support .
After equipping Late and Late , remove Stormblast Mine and Minefield Support .
This is to reduce the amount of buttons you need to press, the damage increase the extra jewel socket slots provide are about the same.
At this point you are Poison damage capped outside of Uber encounters and Simulacrum without Unbound Ailments Support .
This means Melee Physical Damage Support is the better support for mapping and non-uber pinnacle fights.
Note : For late Option 1 & 2 use a level 4 Empower Support instead of Unbound Ailments Support .
Replace Multistrike Support with Greater Multistrike Support .
On your Shield Charge you have the following two options to corrupt: Faster Attacks Support Momentum Support On your Steelskin or Despair you want to corrupt a level 1 More Duration Support .
Make sure Despair is linked together with Steelskin if that gem has the support on it.
Note : When you have corrupted More Duration Support , you want to link Enhance Support with your Offering skill.

On your support Animate Guardian or Raise Spectre gem you want to get one of two options: Feeding Frenzy Support (preferred) Summon Phantasm Support (Not recommended) Note : Preferred choice is Animate Guardian .
The reason Summon Phantasm Support is not recommended is because the survivability of all your other Minions drop significantly if a level 1 gem is used instead of 20.

This opens up the possibility to link the skill gem with the new Congregation Support for an extra Spectre such as: Carnage Chieftain (If you have a Perfect Tiger then this would improves the uptime for frenzy charges) Perfect Conjuror of Rot (Malevolence aura) Passives Mirage 3.28 Carrion Golems & Zombies Necromancer League Starter How to Progress After finishing the campaign, you begin interacting with the Atlas of Worlds.

Campaign Tree Going through the campaign, you play using multiple different Minions which means you want to invest skill points into Minion damage, Minion survivability and your own survivability in the form of Life , resistances and block chance.
During the campaign, the most important skill point for Minions survivability is the Minion Defence Mastery " Minions Recover 5% of Life on Minion Death ".
Combine this with Summon Phantasm Support or Summon Raging Spirit to continually heal your Minions.
Spiritual Aid increases your Absolution damage and Spiritual Command boost your Minion's damage while providing you with extra attack speed for Shield Charge .
Sovereignty and the Mana Mastery " 12% increased Mana Reservation Efficiency of Skills " makes it possible to use Purity of Elements , Herald of Purity , Vitality and Clarity .
Two other important jewels to use are One Abyss Jewel with taunt and One Abyss Jewel with Leech .
Note : Remember to respec your Minion Defence Mastery node to Minions have +8 to all maximum elemental resistances AFTER you get the medium cluster jewel.
Make sure to avoid having any kind of Life modifier on your body armour to gain an additional 15% increased maximum Life .

A massive damage increase for the build is to reach 100% chance to Poison with your Minions, for this reason you need to get multiple Ghastly Eye Jewels with the modifier " Minions have #% Chance to Poison Enemies on hit ".
Make sure that at least one of them also has Minions taunt on hit and one with Minions Leech #% of damage as Life .

Here are a few example: Early Option C Early poison with taunt Early poison & leech When you get The Jinxed Juju , anoint Death Attunement on it and then respec your skill tree into the " Early Death Attunement " tree.

Mid Tree To massively increase your Damage and Minion Survivability you need to respec into the "Mid" skill tree and get two Mid 8 passive large cluster jewels with the notables Feasting Fiends and Renewal .
You want to use the unique jewel called Watcher's Eye to increase your Physical damage reduction.
Note : You can have other modifiers on it but you want to prioritize the Physical damage reduction modifier when you have Determination .
Get 5-6 Tattoo of the Valako Shieldbearer to cap your Attack Block Chance.

(These tattoos are also used to manage your Strength vs Intelligence when you have Rational Doctrine ) Improve your Ghastly Eye Jewels to the following: Mid with taunt Option D Option E Note : You don't need an Abyss Jewel with Minion leech now since you have two Feasting Fiends notables on your large clusters.
When you have respecced into Bitter Heresy you need to make up for the lost block chance when you used Mistress of Sacrifice together with Bone Offering .
Late Tree Continue scaling the build with these jewels and uniques: Amanamu's Gaze to massively increase the Poison damage your Minions deal.

Late 8 Passive (Minion Damage, Strength and Maximum Life ) Late 12 passive (Minion Damage, Resistances and Maximum Life ) Late 4-5 passive (Minion Survivability, Minion Leech and Strength) Watcher's Eye ( Physical damage reduction and attack block chance) Note : To be able to fit the new jewels you need to upgrade your Boots and Gloves to have the modifier "Has 1 Abyssal Socket".
You need more Tattoos of the Valako Shieldbearer to cap your attack block chance.
Golem cooldown recovery is more important at this point over golem attack speed.
Commander of Darkness is an Aura that helps you cap your resistances, and those of your Minions while providing damage.
It's a great early game choice, but later you swap it out because you can make up for the damage and resistances with gear.
Unnatural Strength converts your Minions Physical damage to Chaos damage.
This means all your sources of damage are scaled by the withered on hit effect and Void Manipulation Support .
Bone Barrier gives you passive Life recovery as your Minions deal damage.
In addition it provides you with Physical damage reduction and gives a significant bump to your Minion's survivability.
Early Game Respec : In the Early Game when you have more resistances on your items, you respec from Commander of Darkness to Mistress of Sacrifice .

Defensive Scaling Sufficient +#% to Fire , Cold , Lightning and Chaos Resistance to cap your Resistances.
Campaign Early Mid Late Gearing Mirage 3.28 Carrion Golems & Zombies Necromancer League Starter Resistances You can get Resistances where v er you want and they do not have to be on specific items.
Make sure to check your Resistances in your Character Sheet (C, Defense Tab) whenever you switch your gear around.
To learn more about defences in Path of Exile, check out our guide here !
Item bases You want to focus on using either Hybrid Armour and Energy Shield or Pure Energy Shield bases.
Armour improves your defensive layer against Physical damage while Energy Shield increases the total damage you can take from all sources except Chaos damage.
Campaign Early Mid Late During the campaign, try to get Life and Resistances on every non-weapon item.
Look for Calling Wand with Increased Minion Damage or Minions have increased Attack and Cast speed .
You can use the crafting bench to craft any extra Resistances for 1 Orb of Transmutation , this works on normal rarity items as well.
Equip Two-Stone Rings after level 20, and get your Fire , Cold , and Lightning Resistances as close to 75+ as possible.

Changelog Forged Frostbearer Spectre Necromancer League Starter for Path of Exile Mirage 3.28 by Helm Breaker Mirage 3.28 Last Updated: July 1, 2026 Follow Changelog FAQ Get Link Advertisement This website is supported by ads.
The Forged Frostbearers cast Cold mortar projectiles that scale very well when supported by Greater Volley Support , since the projectiles overlap (an effect known as shotgunning).
This interaction makes it so that these Spectres deal a lot of single target damage against enemies while also having decent clear speed from the AoE splash damage.
Early on you play a Life and Energy Shield hybrid build with high attack and spell block chance.
Later, you switch over to a pure Energy Shield setup with Chaos Inoculation and 100% Spell Suppression while also keeping the high chance to block.
Later on you need to get a The Dark Monarch , Bonemeld and Bitterbind Point +1 Spectre to min-max your endgame damage.

Forged Frostbearer Spectre Necromancer League Starter Gear Set Examples Resistances and Attributes You do not need specific resistances and attributes on any item, just get the required total resistances and attributes amongst all gear combined.

Note : If you do get invited by a fellow Minion player you go to their hideout and then remove Bone Offering or Flesh Offering from your trigger wand first and do not have Spell Echo Support linked to Raise Spectre .
Bone Offering massively improves the survivability of your Minions and it provides you with a lot of block chance thanks to Mistress of Sacrifice Ascendancy node.
Replace Conductivity with Frostbite since Forged Frostbearers deal Cold damage.
When you use a trigger wand for Bone Offering and Desecrate make sure you keep Desecrate at level 1 to reduce mana cost.
You need minimum one Ghastly eye jewel with the Cold damage modifier to be able to inflicit Cold exposure on enemies with your Raise Zombies when they are supported by Elemental Army .
Note : Avoid Ghastly Eye Jewels with Fire and Lightning damage modifiers.
The Turtle provides a 5% flat physical damage reduction buff and a weaker Determination aura.
Note : Make sure to remove Bone Offering from your trigger wand first and do not have Spell Echo Support linked to Raise Spectre while raising your Spectres.
In the Mid skill gem setup you need to get Stormblast Mines and support it with Minefield Support .
This skill is only used to debuff tanky rares and bosses so that they take 4% increased damage per nearby mine.
In the Late setup when you switch to using Chaos Inoculation you want to anoint Whispers of Doom on your amulet and use Sniper's Mark instead of Stormblast Mines .
When you obtain Bitterbind Point +1 Spectre support your Steelskin with a level one Arcane Surge Support to buff your Spectres.
In the Late skill gem setup you need to remove Meat Shield Support from your support Spectres and instead use Congregation Support .
With Congregation Support the maximum number of Spectres you can summon increases to 16.
11 of them should be Forged Frostbearers and 5 of them are support Spectres.
The new support Spectre is a Perfect Primal Thunderbird which provides you with Grace and a chance to shock enemies for extra damage.
Note : If you cannot afford it, you can instead use Bonechill Support together with a Primal Crushclaw Spectre.
You need to get either a level 4 Empower Support or a Cold Penetration Support for your main damage Spectres.
Note : If you use Empower Support you can gem swap it with Annihilation Support for improved clear speed in maps.
When you have Plaguebringer Forbidden Flesh and Flame, you need to support Desecrate in your trigger wand with Spell Cascade Support .
Replace Spell Echo Support with Greater Spell Echo Support .
On your Shield Charge you have the following two options to corrupt: Faster Attacks Support Momentum Support On your Steelskin you want to imbue it with Arcane Surge Support .
If you dont have Arcane Surge Support imbued, you cannot use More Duration Support or Cooldown Recovery Support in the Late setup.

On your support Raise Spectre gem you want to get one of two options: Elemental Army Support ( LATE SETUP ONLY ) Feeding Frenzy Support ( LATE SETUP ONLY ) Swap your Elemental Army Support or Feeding Frenzy Support out for Bonechill Support .
When you have Bonechill Support you need to replace a Forged Frostbearer with a Primal Crushclaw Spectre.

The Primal Crushclaw debuffs enemies with 20% increased Cold damage taken and together with Bonechill Support enemies take extra Cold damage depending on the chill effect applied by your Primal Crushclaw .
Note : In the Mid setup, you need to have one level 21 corrupted Raise Spectre gem for your support Spectres, to be able to summon 10 Spectres in total.
Spiritual Aid increases your Absolution damage and Spiritual Command is taken to increase the damage of your Minions.
Sovereignty and the Mana Mastery " 12% increased Mana Reservation Efficiency of Skills " is taken to make it possible to use Purity of Elements , Herald of Purity , Vitality and Clarity .
Two other important jewel to use is one Early taunt with the Taunt modifier and one One with Leech with the Leech modifier.
Mid Game Tree You need to get one large Minion damage cluster jewel like this Mid and one Fortress Covenant for increased Minion damage and Minion survivability.
To increase your own survivability against physical damage you need to get one Watcher's Eye with the modifier " #% Additional Physical Damage Reduction while affected by Determination ".

Late Game Tree Once you have enough Energy Shield items you switch to using the following Keystones: Chaos Inoculation makes you immune to all Chaos damage and removes the need of having Chaos resistance on items.
Ghost Reaver changes the Life leech from Bone Barrier to now leech Energy Shield instead.
Zealot's Oath changes your Life regeneration into Energy Shield regeneration instead.

To further scale your own survivability and Minion damage you need to get these cluster jewels and uniques: Late 1 Small (Provides you with Energy Shield , Resistances and Spell Suppression) Late 1 Large (Minion damage, Attributes and Energy Shield ) Watcher's Eye (Two good modifiers) Foulborn The Blue Dream with the modifier Passives granting Lightning Resistance or all Elemental Resistances in Radius also grant increased Maximum Energy Shield at 75% of its value .
You want to use one Runegraft of the Fortress on your Spell Suppression Mastery by Inveterate .
If you have enough elemental resistances from your items you want to replace the Commander of Darkness ascendancy node for the Catarina Bloodline Umbral Army node.
This gives you +120-160% Increased Defences with 12-16 Spectres .

Additional upgrades can be made in the Late ES 2x Clusters & Late Flesh and Flame Jewels skill trees: Late 2 Large (Minion damage, Attributes and Energy Shield ) Late 2 Small (Provides you with Energy Shield , Attributes and Spell Suppression) You want the unique jewels called Forbidden Flame and Forbidden Flesh with one of the following modifiers: 1.
Note : If you have Forbidden Flame and Flesh, you need to imbue Desecrate with Spell Cascade Support or replace Inspiration Support with Spell Cascade Support .
Commander of Darkness This is an aura that helps in capping resistances for you and your minions while providing damage.
Mindless Aggression provides more Life and damage to your Minions.
Mistress of Sacrifice applies Bone Offering providing you with both attack and spell block chance.
In addition it provides you with physical damage reduction and gives a significant bump to your Minion's survivability.

Late Game Respec : In the Late Game when you switch to Chaos Inoculation and have more resistances on your items, you respec from Commander of Darkness to using the new Catarina Bloodline Umbral Army node.

Item bases In the early game, you want to focus on using either Hybrid Armour and Energy Shield or Pure Energy Shield bases.
