Changelog Cyclone Elementalist League Starter for Path of Exile Mirage 3.28 by AoinoMiku Mirage 3.28 Last Updated: March 24, 2026 Follow Changelog FAQ Get Link Advertisement This website is supported by ads.
Unlike the other physical Cyclone builds, the Elementalist Cyclone focuses on dealing damage with all the elemental types and using elemental ailments to debuff and debilitate its opponents.

When reaching endgame, this build completely unlocks all the chains holding it back with The Fulcrum , allowing us to not only affect our enemies with Elemental Ailments, but also reflect them back on to us.
Cyclone Elementalist League Starter Gear Resistances You do not need specific resistances on any item, just get the required total resistances amongst all gear combined.

Skills Mirage 3.28 Cyclone Elementalist League Starter Rolling Magma Storm Brand Cyclone Void Shockwave Cyclone of Tumult Rolling Magma has great damage and is our starter skill until we reach level 12.
Clarity and Vitality are used early on, as they provide us with some HP and Mana regen for cheap.
Orb of Storms is used for additional damage to help on Bosses and tougher encounters.
We are also using a multitude of buffs to increase our damage, like Herald of Thunder , Herald of Ice and Wrath .
4 Golems can also be used to buff us after we finish the first Lab, they are: Summon Lightning Golem , Summon Flame Golem , Summon Stone Golem , and Summon Chaos Golem .
We also start running a Cast when Damage Taken Support setup with Immortal Call .
For Auras, we swap Summon Skitterbots for Herald of Ice , Precision and Blood and Sand , as these are better to support Cyclone with.
For your Golems you have a choice; you need to swap in Summon Ice Golem , so either Summon Stone Golem or Summon Chaos Golem have to go.
Personally, Chaos Resistance feels a bit more valuable most of the time, so it's recommended to remove Summon Stone Golem .
We are now running a Mark On Hit Support setup with Assassin's Mark so we can maintain Power Charges on Bosses to deal more damage to them.
Blood Rage is a great support tool for any attack skill, as it provides attack speed and an easy way to obtain Frensy Charges.
If you want a bit more tankiness without losing much damage you can swap Enhance Support and Empower Support for Cast when Damage Taken Support with Immortal Call .

Void Shockwave Support increases our clear immensely and is a must take, in this guide we are replacing Elemental Damage with Attacks Support but it is also viable to replace Infused Channelling Support instead, if you want slightly more damage; Infused Channelling Support provides 12% less damage taken and, since it is a different colour, can not be easily swapped for Void Shockwave Support when you are dealing with Bosses.
Cyclone of Tumult can be used instead of the normal Cyclone to maximize your damage at the cost of some speed.
Coin Corruptions Skill Gems may Brick Make sure to always have a replacement Skill Gem ready to go before you Slam your main Skill Gem as certain supports may make it unusable for your current build.
Cyclone best pairs with the following Coin Corruptions: Offensive: Increased Critical Damage Support , Hypothermia Support , Bonechill Support .
This results in an absurd amount of buffs super early, which allow us to easily stay alive and deal tons more damage.
Mastermind of Discord improves the exposures we apply, making even the smallest one a big deal, and it also gives us some sweet extra mana regen.
Heart of Destruction enhances our clear and helps improve our damage on Bosses or difficult enemies encountered in maps.
It is fine to keep Storm Brand for a bit until you manage to grab enough currency to swap.
Make sure to do a lot of Chaos Recipes , as it not only helps for getting enough initial currency to swap to Cyclone , but also for saving up to eventually grab a The Fulcrum .
It is not mandatory, but you take a lot of extra damage without it.

Stat Priorities Mirage 3.28e Cyclone Elementalist League Starter Learn more If you're struggling to survive, progress or want to learn more about damage and defenses in Path of Exile check out our Getting Started section.
Weapon Until you swap to The Fulcrum , upgrading your Staff is the best way for you to improve your damage.
Item Bases While leveling, focus on wearing hybrid Armour and Energy Shield bases.
You mostly care about the Armour to reduce some of the damage you are taking, but you also need your bases to have a lot of blue sockets so you can easily socket all your gems.
When you get to early Maps, you can start looking more into hybrid Armor and Evasion bases.
As long as you can fulfill your socket needs, these bases give you the best overall damage mitigation.
Try and get your Elemental Resistances as close to 75% as you can; remember to use the Crafting Bench in your Hideout as needed.
Try and do your first Lab as soon as you can, as that boosts your damage and defenses tremendously.
The rest of your gear is mostly there to give you Life rolls, cap your resists and meet your attribute requirements.
Make sure to get your Cluster Jewels before you change your amulet anointment to not lose access to Leech.
On your Lethal Pride make sure to look for Chance to deal Double Damage and Melee Critical Strike Multiplier.
Cyclone Elementalist Endgame Gear With the introduction of Mageblood the build becomes way tankier, faster and gains a bit extra damage.
Remember to also invest in a new set of Flasks as that is where Mageblood 's Power comes from.
Death Rush provides Adrenaline to the build giving it a trinity of benefits, from damage to movement speed and even Physical damage reduction.
Prioritize "+% to all Maximum Elemental Resistances" over the offensive stats, as at this point damage should not be that much of an issue.
Upgrade your rares and try to swap your gear to Armour only Bases.
Inpulsa's Broken Heart can now be replaced by a rare chest piece with the explode mod from the Crusader and the reduced mana cost of attacks from the Warlord.

Otherwise, avoid the following modifiers: Monsters cannot be Leeched from Monsters reflect 18% of Elemental Damage Players have (-12--9)% to all maximum Resistances Monsters have 70% chance to Avoid Elemental Ailments Although "Monsters reflect 18% of Physical Damage" is not mentioned above, it can give you some trouble early on before you swap to Fulcrum, as you will be doing small amounts of Physical Damage.
The build self-inflicts itself with elemental ailments and takes advantage of that to acquire strong buffs.
Swapped Aura Gems around to better benefit from the local reservation and tweaked their levels.

Changelog Boneshatter Juggernaut League Starter for Path of Exile Mirage 3.28 by Zizaran Mirage 3.28 Last Updated: March 23, 2026 Follow Changelog FAQ Get Link Advertisement This website is supported by ads.
The Juggernaut ascendancy provides you with immunity to slows, good endurance charge uptime and tons of regeneration.
Stack Trauma with Boneshatter of Complex Trauma to ramp your damage up.
Aggravating your bleeds with nodes like Bloodletting makes sure enemies always count as moving, tripling the damage they take from bleeding.
Having 9 Endurance Charges nearly halves your physical and elemental damage taken.
On top of that you get a good chunk of armour which, when combined with Molten Shell adds another layer of defense.
Fortification provides you with 21% less damage taken, and is active as long as you've hit an enemy recently.
Nodes like Untiring provide you with a lot of recovery, so any damage you DO take is quickly regained.
Boneshatter Juggernaut Gear Resistances You do not need specific resistances on any item, just get the required total resistances amongst all gear combined.

From level 4 to 12 you have a choice: The most efficient way to play is using Spectral Throw , try to keep enemies at just under the maximum range to deal maximum damage, because this increases the likelyhood you get 3 hits per throw.
You can also swap Volley Support with Faster Attacks Support on bosses for more single target.
Intimidating Cry doubles your single target damage for two attacks, use it whenever it's off-cooldown during boss fights.
Boneshatter of Complex Trauma is your main damage skill.
Using this skill gives you up to 9 stacks of Trauma, each granting you extra damage.
Your Trauma resets to 0 when you reach 10 Trauma, while it's technically ideal to stay at exactly 9 Trauma all the time, this is hard to manage.
When you follow this build Trauma mostly manages itself assuming you're not literally standing still hitting a boss, and even if it falls off you are back at 9 stacks in no time.
Untiring is how you sustain the self-hit damage from Boneshatter of Complex Trauma .
Since your physical damage mitigation is 90% this node means you heal more life than you take in damage from the Boneshatter self-hit.
This quickly ramps up providing you with a lot of regeneration.
Getting Resolute Technique early makes sure accuracy is never an issue.
Your focus for this tree is getting 100% chance to bleed, you also pick up Spirit of War so you leech mana and have an easier time sustaining your skill costs.
This tree can also use a The Light of Meaning , Either #% increased Physical Damage or Chaos Resistance work, but Physical Damage is preferred.
If you're looking to use a Watcher's Eye , the only mod that's useful is "#% increased Physical Damage while using Pride ".
This won't beat a jewel with ailments deal damage faster and Physical Damage over Time Multiplier, but if it's cheap it's worth picking up.
This jewel gives you a hefty chunk of Energy Shield, which enables you to use Divine Shield for even more recovery.
Path of Building has a "Find Timeless Jewel" function that you can use to find the best seed for a Glorious Vanity .

Stat Priorities 3.28 - Mirage Boneshatter Juggernaut League Starter Learn more If you're struggling to survive, progress or want to learn more about damage and defenses in Path of Exile check out our Getting Started section.
Armour It is very important to itemise your character with as much Armour as possible as it ensures the build feels comfortable to play.
Prioritising the Highest level item bases for every slot sets your build up to have as high baseline defences as possible.
Early Maps Endgame In early maps your main focus should be on getting your resistances capped and having life on every piece of gear.
Kaom's Sign is a cheap unique you can pick up early on that greatly improves your defenses.
Use only 1 of these since in the early game there's a lot of pressure to get resists on your other ring.
Don't neglect your flasks, you don't need perfect ones early on, but try to get some way to remove corrupting blood at the very least.
Try to pick one up with a Physical Damage top-end as close to 800 as possible(or over).
Ryslatha's Coil provides you with roughly 40-50% more damage.
By now your flasks and tincture should be (near) perfect.
You take 10% reduced Extra Damage from Critical Strikes per Endurance Charge on your chest is most easily achieved by fracturing it and then rolling prefixes by spamming dense fossils.

Otherwise, avoid the following modifiers: Monsters reflect 18% of Physical Damage Monsters have a 50% chance to avoid Poison, Impale, and Bleeding (25-30)% more Monster Life · Monsters cannot be Stunned Monsters steal Power, Frenzy and Endurance charges on Hit When do I pick up Divine Shield?
You should be taking some damage when attacking(that's how the build works), but you should be regenerating it very fast.
First things to check are whether your Endurance Charges(at least 7) are up and whether you clicked a -#% Physical Damage Reduction Eater of Worlds altar.
If your charges are up and you didn't click any altar, try to get some more Armour.
With the amount of recovery you have, you effectively take 40% less damage from hits.

Skills 3.28 - Mirage Kinetic Fusillade Ballista Hierophant League Starter Level 1-40 Kinetic Fusillade Respec Endgame Skills From Level 1 to 40, you use fire spells and spell totems to deal damage.
Kill Hillock, get to town and stash your Momentum Support .
Momentum Support is usually not available on Templar and gives you a huge attack speed bonus for your Shield Charge .
Rolling Magma is your main clear and damage dealer.
It applies Ignites and spreads them with the help of Elemental Proliferation Support .
Holy Flame Totem is your supplementary damage of choice.
It has both good damage and clear and is used to substitute Rolling Magma throughout leveling.
Flame Wall grants huge damage to every Projectile that travels through it.
Elemental Weakness debuffs harder enemies, lowering their resistances and therefore increasing your damage.
Clarity helps you with mana, but make sure you don't level it too high or your reservation might be too high.
Whenever you first get to the Library, buy the following Gems: Kinetic Fusillade , Frenzy , Less Duration Support , Ballista Totem Support and Faster Attacks Support .
Especially Ballista Totem Support , because its life scales with Levels.
Use Precision with Arrogance Support to reserve Life instead of Mana.
Flame Wall grants you extra fire damage when Totems fire through it.
Use Frenzy to get Frenzy Charges and apply the Elemental Weakness curse with Cast On Critical Strike Support .
Molten Shell supported with Cast when Damage Taken Support makes sure you get a huge Damage Absorption Shield whenever needed the most.
Make sure to get as much Armour as possible to make this skill more useful.

To reduce the Duration of the Skill use: Less Duration Support , make sure to have this fully leveled and at 20% quality as soon as you can From the Passive Tree: Window of Opportunity , the Reduced Skill Duration nodes leading up to it and the Duration Wheel Mastery for 10% less skill effect duration.
since Ballista Totem Support also starts with 50% less attack speed.
These are low-investment options and can increase damage significantly.
This is the more expensive version, because the base-item is harder to get, and you have to craft it yourself using Essences, Fossils or Chaos Orbs .
If you need the damage, refresh your Frenzy Charges every so often using Frenzy .
This is also how you apply Elemental Weakness against highly resistant rares.
Then, Ritual of Awakening increases your damage while also fixing your Life and Mana Sustain.
Endurance Charges help you tank big hits, while the Power Charges increase your Critical Strike Chance.
Esh of the Storm needs you to redesign the build to only deal Lightning Damage on Wand, Jewelry and you swap everything that has to do with Anger to Wrath .
Now you can use a Beast corpse, for example Perfect Forest Tiger and summon it, which grants you the Beasts Aura as well as 100% increased Damage, 3% Life Regenerated per Second and Stun Immunity.
You can swap it temporarily to Soul of Ryslatha for Bossing, in case you need sustain for your Life Flasks.
For level 1-40, Your wand dictates most of your damage.
Make sure it has either +1 to level of fire spell skills or a high amount of added damage to spells .
In there is a Crafting Recipe for added fire damage to spells .
Once you respec to the Crit Version, make sure you unallocate Elemental Overload , otherwise you are sabotaging your build.
Unfortunately, the tree doesn't provide you with much Armour, so you have to get a %increased armour roll on your Granite Flask as soon as possible.
This also means that the pressure is on your gear to deliver defenses, prioritize a ton of armour on all of your gear pieces!
After getting Glancing Blows , you can unspec out of a lot of Spell Block nodes on the tree, including Arcane Guarding and Safeguard .

Maps Endgame Gearing 3.28 - Mirage Kinetic Fusillade Ballista Hierophant League Starter Resistances You can get Resistances where v er you want and they do not have to be on specific items.
Campaign Maps Endgame Aspirational During the campaign, try to keep your Elemental Resistances as high as possible.
For Level 1-40, since you start as a caster, your wand will dictate most of your damage.
Make sure it has either +1 to level of fire skill spells or a high amount of added damage to spells .
Next to Dialla in the Eternal Lab (left side) in Act3, there is a Crafting Recipe for added fire damage to spells .

In order to start Kinetic Fusillade Ballista, you NEED a 4-Link with the right colours: RR B G In order to get the right colours and links if you haven't found one, use Faustus to gamble for one Armour/Evasion Body Armour.
Then use Chromatic Orb s on an Armour/Evasion or Armour/Energy Shield basetype with 4-links.
On your Wand you want as much Added Fire/Lightning/Cold Damage as possible.
Fire/Lightning/Cold Damage to spells does NOT work.
However, %increased Spell damage is a decent stat because Kinetic Fusillade converts it to Attack Damage.
If you get a bad wand it's not the end of the world, just make sure that you at least have an Added Fire/Lightning/Cold Damage craft from your Crafting Bench.
Upgrade the Wand as soon as you can, it's your main damage dealer.
To substitute your damage, get Added Fire/Cold/Lightning Damage from your Rings, Amulet and Gloves.
All the above Damage crafts need Orb of Transmutation , so make sure you always vendor your Magic and Rare Items unidentified.
Kinetic Fusillade Ballista Hierophant Campaign Gear As you enter the Atlas of Worlds and begin mapping, your first goal is to cap your resistances and meet Attribute requirements.
Make sure to check your Accuracy regularly as you level up, since enemy Evasion goes up with Map Tiers!
Get a Doedre's Tenure as soon as possible for a cheap damage boost.
Try to get your Armour up from gear and by upgrading one of your Flasks with an %increased armour during effect mod.
Getting Chaos Resistance early can be expensive, but you can help this with hybrid Resistance crafts.
If you need damage, get a Shield with % Spell Damage.
If you need Defense, get a high armour Shield with Life.
In both cases, you want as high % block chance as possible.
You definitely want %critical strike chance and possible % increased attack speed.
If you can, finish with a attacks with this weapon penetrate % resistances craft.
Otherwise, check the trade market for cheap relevant dps upgrades.
High Armour on every gear piece, with some Energy Shield mixed in, especially on your Body Armour.

Changelog Cyclone Shockwave Slayer League Starter for Path of Exile Mirage 3.28 by Cptn Garbage Mirage 3.28 Last Updated: March 6, 2026 Follow Changelog FAQ Get Link Advertisement This website is supported by ads.
Shockwaves from Shockwave Support create large, damaging Area of Effect pulses whenever your Cyclone hits an enemy, providing you with comfortable screen clear.
You may however easily switch to Tumult for its higher base damage, at the cost of lower movement speed.
Simply equip The Stampede or Replica Stampede and replace Death Rush with a Rare Ring to fix resistances instead.
Also replace the Cyclone in your General's Cry with Cyclone of Tumult as your clones inherit your Tumult stacks for bonus damage.
Cyclone Shockwave Slayer League Starter Gear Resistances You do not need specific resistances on any item, just get the required total resistances amongst all gear combined.
Tip to maximize damage: Position yourself so the projectiles return point is inside the monster.
This allows it to hit up to 4 times instead of the usual 2 to maximize single target damage.
Use Frostblink and Leap Slam to quickly move around Reserve Precision by activating it on your Skill bar for a boost to Accuracy.
Your maximum Mana should go down when done correctly, unless you are in Town.
Link Shockwave Support to Cyclone in order to massively boost your burst damage and area coverage.
Important: While Cyclone can be used with any melee weapon, Cyclone supported with Shockwave cannot.
This means in order to use Shockwave Support you must use a Mace or Staff.
If you find a really good Axe or Sword during the Campaign, it's okay to sub out Shockwave for another Support gem until you find a Mace or Staff.
Though keep in mind Shockwave Support is very strong so slightly worse, but compatible weapons, should be prioritized.
General's Cry linked to Cyclone and Autoexertion perfectly supplements your damage output by periodically creating clones of yourself, which then use Cyclone on the nearest enemy before disappearing.
The 50% increased Accuracy Rating makes it easier to satisfy the Precise Technique condition.
Strike Range and AoE per kill increases the AoE of your Cyclone later on and 15% more damage is 15% more damage.
Bane of Legends grants a powerful generic boost in damage and makes you immune to reflected damage in Endgame Maps.
This causes your life leech to continue in the background for up to 20 seconds, even when at full life.
It also grants generic 10% reduced damage taken while leeching, which is effectively always.
Headsman causes any Enemy that would fall below 20% HP to die instantly, resulting in a 25% DPS increase (Why 25%?
A 50% cull would half the monster's HP, which would effectively double your damage).
Campaign Tree The +20 Int from Mana Flows allows you to use Frostblink .
The Fire Mastery at Burning Brutality grants a lot of extra damage against ignited enemies.
Only fire damage can ignite, so make sure to run Herald of Ash for the Physical Damage as extra Fire Damage, as otherwise the invested points are purely cosmetic.
Precise Technique requires your Accuracy to be higher than your maximum Life to grant a massive 40% more damage.
While very powerful, it's not uncommon to forget to keep up with the required Accuracy.
Occasionally check your "Main Hand Accuracy Rating" in your Character Panel to make sure you're still enjoying 40% more damage.
The rest of the tree is straight forward as you simply pick up various sources of damage, mana sustain and life as you spin through the campaign.
Crit Swap You eventually prune the right side of the Skill Tree to free up points that go towards Crit instead.

Here's a checklist of things you should have before the switch: Level 85+ A high physical DPS staff with an "#% increased Critical Strike Chance" roll Assassin's Mark linked to Mark On Hit Support and Lifetap Support A Diamond Flask and a "#% increased Critical Strike Chance during Effect" roll on any of your flasks.
Abyssus grants a LOT of extra damage, but does make you more susceptible to Physical Damage.
Use it unless you prefer to be more durable at the expense of a lot of extra damage.
Later on, once your damage is more than enough, you can switch the Abyssus for a Rare helmet instead.
3 Notable Physical Damage Large Clusters are worse than the shown Staff cluster but still very good.
IMPORTANT: This tree unallocates both Mana Cost wheels.
Keep these until you have your Double Influenced Body Armour for the -20 Cost mod.

Item Bases It is very important to itemise your character with as much Armour as possible as it ensures the build feels comfortable to play.
Campaign Early Maps Crit Swap Cluster Endgame Super Endgame Choices During the campaign, try to get life and Resistances on every non-weapon item.

Regularly upgrade your Weapon by checking Vendors for higher tier bases (which have substantially higher base damage), set your item filter up to highlight promising bases to identify, gamble for them at the respective Vendors in town for Gold or buy them from other players.
The rarity of the Rustic Sash determines how much % increased Physical Damage the weapon you get in return has.
You can use the Crafting Bench to craft any extra Resistances for 1 Orb of Transmutation .
This works on normal (white) rarity items as well and is a quick way to quickly shore up your resistances.
Equip Two-Stone Rings after level 20, and get your Fire , Cold and Lightning Resistances as close to 75%+ as possible.
Cyclone Shockwave Slayer Campaign Gear As you enter the Atlas of Worlds and begin mapping, your first goal is to cap your resistances and meet Attribute requirements.
Your first goal should be to check whether your Weapon is adequate, as it's the largest contributor to your overall damage output.
Prioritize getting a 6-link Body Armour with high Armour values.

Try to not have any life modifiers such as +# to Maximum Life or Life Regeneration as it disables the very strong "15% increased maximum Life if there are no Life modifiers on equipped Body Armour" Life Mastery .
Aim for Armour on your other pieces too, but prioritize high Life, Resistances and Movement Speed on your boots.

Pick up an early Rumi's Concoction to boost your Block Chance Here are a few other optional Uniques to look out for, though some may be expensive: Carnage Heart for a good mix between Attributes, Resistances and damage.
A good enough Amulet to anoint Enigmatic Defence on.
Ryslatha's Coil as an alternative high damage belt.
Death Rush for a generically powerful boost of Damage, movement speed and damage reduction thanks to Adrenaline.
Cyclone Shockwave Slayer Early Map Gear Here's a checklist of things you should have before the switch: Level 90+ A high physical DPS staff with an "#% increased Critical Strike Chance" roll.
Assassin's Mark linked to Mark On Hit Support and Lifetap Support .
A Diamond Flask and a "#% increased Critical Strike Chance during Effect" Suffix on any of your flasks.

Make sure to have the basic flask affixes at this point: increased Armour increased Crit Chance increased Movement Speed Immunity to Corrupted Blood (Life Flask) Cyclone Shockwave Slayer Crit Swap Gear Switch to a Cluster Jewel Setup once you have similar Clusters as shown in the guide.

Body Armour: A high Armour double influenced Hunter-Warlord Royal Plate Stat priority for the Hunter-Warlord Chest: You can apply an additional Curse Socketed Attacks have -20 to Total Mana Cost Attacks have +#% to Critical Strike Chance High Armour Values Physical damage taken as X Additional Physical Damage Reduction A regular Eldritch Influenced Chest with 6000+ Armour and % Additional Physical Damage Reduction is also a good choice before you craft the influenced Body Armour.
Use a Physical Damage or Chaos Resistance The Light of Meaning to gain more damage or help with suffix pressure, respectively.
Look for a Lethal Pride with as many sources of Double damage, crit multi, melee damage and other useful stats.

Here are other good mods to pair it with: #% chance to Impale Enemies on Hit with Attacks while using Pride #% chance to deal Double Damage while using Pride #% increased Physical Damage while using Pride #% increased Physical Damage while using Precision #% increased Attack Speed while affected by Precision +#% to Critical Strike Multiplier while affected by Precision There are two great options for Forbidden Flame : More Than Skill and Master of Metal Progenesis is expensive but very strong.
Belt: Mageblood , Headhunter and the new Shrine belt Screams of the Desiccated Mageblood is the best choice if you value unconditional movement speed and defenses as well as easier gearing.
Consider substituting Brutality Support with Increased Critical Damage Support as Headhunter buffs grant a lot of extra elemental damage.
Soul Ascension pairs better with Mageblood as Resistances get very tight otherwise.
A 2 mod Reflected Focused Amulet easily beats Stranglegasp, though requires investment in Block Chance elsewhere.
If your goal is merely chasing DPS numbers, Abyssus is impossible to beat.
Though at a certain point your DPS is more than plenty without having to give up durability for it.

Changelog Frost Blades Slayer League Starter for Path of Exile Mirage 3.28 by FuzzyDuckzy Mirage 3.28 Last Updated: March 5, 2026 Follow Changelog FAQ Get Link Advertisement This website is supported by ads.
Use Frost Blades from the very start to end game as your main damage dealing skill.
Early on, use Precise Technique combined with a fire mastery to add 100% increased damage to ignited foes, which you achieve through Added Fire Damage Support .
Mid acts, you add Hatchet Master for more damage and clear speed.
Use swords early, as they have good attack speed and an accuracy implicit.
Continue using axes through the campaign and early maps until you hit level 78 and can then swap to White Wind and the critical strike variant.
Frost Blades Slayer Gear Resistances You do not need specific resistances on any item, just get the required total resistances amongst all gear combined.
Before you get +1 strike on the passive tree in act 2, use Ancestral Call Support when clearing zones and swap out for Ruthless Support on Bosses.
Bane of Legends gives a nice early damage boost and makes you immune to Physical Reflect.
Do not be tempted to take Brutal Fervour first as you do not have the damage early on to leech life.
Headsman gives boosts to both damage and speed when mapping and gives a whopping 20% Culling Strike, which is very useful when taking on Pinnacle Bosses.
Brutal Fervour doubles life leech and gives overleech, which ensures that even when not attacking you have a strong source of life recovery if you have attacked an enemy recently.
Masterful Form gives huge damage and defensive boosts.
To ensure this is always up, check your accuracy regularly and, if accuracy starts to get lower than your life, equip a low level Precision .
When you are ready to switch to the crit version, you need at least 131 intelligence, so try to find this on jewellery or temporarily use +30 Int nodes.

You need to be 100% immune to elemental ailments when equipping The Arkhon's Tools Stat Priorities 3.28 Mirage Frost Blades Slayer League Starter Learn more If you're struggling to survive, progress or want to learn more about damage and defenses in Path of Exile check out our Getting Started section.
Offensive Scaling Scaling Tags: Strike, Cold, Attack, Elemental, Critical Strike Chance, Critical Strike Multiplier, Melee.
You can also use harvest to swap the elemental resistance on items when you are in endgame Rare gear in this section is easier to craft if you obtain a Fractured base.

Pay attention to the eldritch mods (implicits) on rare items as they are all needed to play the build smoothly at end game Fortify Use Fortify Support in your 6 link instead of Elemental Damage with Attacks Support until you can get a chest with Fortify on hit Implicit (Eater of Worlds Modifiers).
Armour It is very important to itemise your character with as much Armour & Evasion as possible.
It is also preferable to have no life rolls on your body armour so you can utilise the 15% Life Mastery.

Look for the following bases for your rare items: Marshall's Brigandine Conquest Lamellar Chimerascale Gauntlets Wyvernscale Gauntlets Rare Jewellery It is important to get high attribute rolls to Strength & Intelligence on your Amulet along with Life and an open Prefix to craft 'Damage while leeching' or open suffix to craft elemental resistances.
Amethyst rings can be easily crafted by buying a base with fractured life or resistances and then using reforge Chaos in Harvest.
Use the POB timeless jewel finder feature to search for specific jewels.
If you find a Prismweave , this is a great source of early damage, but do not spend currency trading for this item.
Use a Two-Handed Sword for Acts 1-2, as they generally have better attack speed and an accuracy implicit .
Upgrade your weapon every couple of Acts, looking for Axes with % physical damage.
Elemental damage and attack speed are nice bonuses.
This slot is very important for melee builds, as all your damage scales from the base weapon damage.
Only use gold for an Axe if you really need an upgrade or armour/evasion body armours when you need to upgrade to a 4/5 link.
Equip a Prismatic Tincture with penetrates elemental resistances.
The Taming gives elemental damage boosts per ailment on an enemy.
You need some form of added fire damage on gear to ignite.
Heatshiver gives big damage boosts on chilled and frozen enemies.
Push your Critical Strike Multiplier and Critical Strike Chance as high as possible.
Critical Strikes inflict Freeze which increases your damage and survivability.

Otherwise, avoid the following modifiers: Monsters cannot be Leeched from Monsters reflect 18% of Elemental Damage Players have (-12--9)% to all maximum Resistance What item filters should I use with this build?
Why do I not have enough Mana to use all my auras?
What level is different for each player, but as a rule of thumb you want to have at least 80 unreserved Mana.
When you are farming red maps you should find enough Eldritch Ichors to roll Chance to Fortify on Hit on your body armour.
Until this point, you need to run Fortify Support in your links instead of Elemental Damage with Attacks Support .

Changelog Reap Ignite Elementalist League Starter for Path of Exile Mirage 3.28 by Manni Mirage 3.28 Last Updated: March 5, 2026 Follow Changelog FAQ Get Link Advertisement This website is supported by ads.
Due to Elementalist's unique ability to amplify the buffs provided by Summoned Golems, you can progress smoothly at league start with a minimal investment into damage on your gear.
With Shaper of Flames any damage type contributes to ignites, making Obliteration an amazing item for both damage and explosions.
The chaos explosions from Obliteration in turn also ignite enemies themselves, causing a chain reaction of explosions and ignites that annihilates any content with high density of enemies.
Defensively, you can easily gear for high Armour and Energy Shield items, as you only need Resistances and a few Attributes on gear.
A high chance to block and solid regeneration round out your defences and overall form a well-rounded league starter build.
Reap Ignite Elementalist Build Guide Example Gear Resistances You do not need specific resistances on any item, just get the required total resistances amongst all gear combined.

Reap Ignite Elementalist Build Guide POB Link Skills 3.27 - Keepers of the Flame Reap Ignite Elementalist League Starter 1-28 Leveling Rolling Magma 28-55 Leveling Firestorm Reap Ignite Endgam e You are required to finish the Cruel Labyrinth before being able to swap to the Reap Ignite Build.
Shooting through Flame Wall supplements your damage.
Dropping a Holy Flame Totem occasionally helps for clear and boss damage.
At level 28, swap the Rolling Magma for a Firestorm and use it for clearing and damage.
At level 34, acquire the first ascendancy and add Summon Flame Golem , Summon Stone Golem , Summon Lightning Golem and Summon Chaos Golem .
Even though ignites last for around 4-5 seconds, you still want to repeatedly cast Reap to maintain Blood Charges and to fish for a critical strike to enable Elemental Overload .
Vaal Reap is your button for extra single target damage.
Not only does Vaal Reap hit harder than regular Reap , it also sets your maximum Blood charges from 5 to 9 for an 8 second duration, resulting in significantly higher DPS for regular Reap .
Arcanist Brand automatically casts Wave of Conviction (exposure), Flammability and Flame Surge to improve the single target DPS.
Tempest Shield , Herald of Ash , Herald of Purity and Determination are your Mana Reservation skills.
Make sure that enough Mana is unreserved to be able to cast Flammability .
Change the Determination to Malevolence once you reach 75% chance to Block Attacks to push damage further.
Shaper of Flames is the second ascendancy pick and enables you to play ignite with Reap, because all damage type are able to ignite now.
The node also grants 100% chance to ignite and makes enemies convert 40% of their physical damage to fire, which is a massive defensive boon later in the game.
Mastermind of Discord lowers enemies' resistance by an additional 25% when Exposure is applied to them.
At first, only enemies that you cast Wave of Conviction on will be affected by this, but later a glove implicit will automate this and apply the -25% resistance debuff consistently.
When hitting a rare or boss, the area is conveniently traded for 30% more damage, helping your single target DPS.
The most important upgrade is the immunity to freeze, which allows you to replace a flask that removes freeze for a more useful one.

You grab some Spell Damage from the Witch Start, some Cast Speed from Mental Rapidity and a Fire Mastery for 100% increased hit damage against ignited enemies to make the time before the transition as smooth as possible.
Pre Cluster Jewel setup - Allocate placeholder damage passives such as Heart of Flame and Spiritual Aid with Redemption and Lord of the Dead .
Endgame Tree changes The Runegraft of the Fortress is a nice endgame addition to improve your Armour and Energy Shield values.
The Runegraft of Treachery grants a lot of Reservation efficiency with practically no downside, as your only aura is Determination and enemies' Armour does not matter for an ignite build.
In the very endgame, you want to use The Red Nightmare to fully cap the Attack Block Chance to 75%.

Stat Priorities 3.27 - Keepers of the Flame Reap Ignite Elementalist League Starter Learn more If you're struggling to survive, progress or want to learn more about damage and defenses in Path of Exile check out our Getting Started section.
this of course includes the Gem Level itself - Gem experience is a massive damage gain early on!
Gain x% of Physical Damage as Extra Fire , Cold , Lightning or Chaos Damage.
All Damage types contribute to the Ignite Damage and are practically equivalent.
Note that Spell Damage and Area Damage do NOT scale the ignite and are therefore mostly worthless.

x% increased Ignite Duration (not a direct single target damage increase, but a helpful stat for clearing.) Increased Golem Buff Effect - scales Damage, Area of Effect, Damage over Time Multiplier and Cast Speed Defensive Scaling Sufficient +#% to Fire , Cold and Lightning Resistance to cap your Resistances.

Maximum Energy Shield and Armour on gear Chance to block Attacks (Chance to block Spells is already capped from the tree alone.) Chaos Resistance ( Summon Chaos Golem provides around 45% already, but you eventually want to add more to cap it.) Increased Golem Buff Effect - scales Armour, Energy Shield, Chaos Resistance and Life Regen.

Endgame Low Budget Reap Ignite Elementalist Build Guide Stat Priorities Endgame Reap Ignite Elementalist Build Guide Stat Priorities Low Budget Gearing 3.27 - Keepers of the Flame Reap Ignite Elementalist League Starter Resistances You can get Resistances where v er you want and they do not have to be on specific items.
Make sure to level the Vaal Gem to a similar level as your current gem before replacing it, otherwise your DPS might drop significantly.
An Obliteration both adds explosions to increase your clearspeed, while also acting as decent DPS upgrade, eliminating the need to buy or craft a rare weapon.
Upgrade it to a perfect roll once you can and eventually hunt for corruptions such as "increased Area of Effect", as the Spell Damage implicit does not increase your Ignite DPS.

First of all, you can replace Ignite Proliferation Support with the stronger Added Fire Damage Support but more importantly, the ignites caused by explosions from Obliteration will now also spread, which in turn causes monsters to die and explode and form a chain-reaction for fast and satisfying clearing.

Cluster Jewels The most important Cluster Jewel is 4 or 5 passive "Burning Damage" Medium Cluster Jewel where Fan the Flames is mandatory and the second notable can be anything from Circling Oblivion , Blowback , Brush with Death , Flow of Life , Student of Decay or Wasting Affliction .
For the Large Cluster, you want an 8-passive "Minion Damage" Large Cluster Jewel for Primordial Bond .
It protects you from Curses that often roll as map modifiers and bolsters your Life Regeneration greatly.
Since Regeneration is your main form of sustain, this ring is very helpful in drawn out encounters.
The Cinderswallow Urn flask not only turns your ignites into a stylish green, but also adds some additional DPS and recovery.
This item has variants for both its recovery on kill modifier, and also for its Veiled modifier.
The ideal recovery modifier is "Recover 3% of Energy Shield on Kill" and the ideal Veiled modifier is "Enemies Ignited by you during Effect have Malediction".
However any variant of this Flask is worth using, so you can start with a cheap one and eventually upgrade to the ideal variant later.
If you can get your hands on a pair of Vaal Caress early, you can use it as a pseudo-6-link before you acquire an actual 6-linked body armour.
A Cloak of Flame can be used as an early, placeholder Body Armour before you acquire a well-rolled Sacred Chainmail .

If you can make up for the loss of Attributes and Resistances on other gear, a Crown of the Inward Eye is a helmet with similar Life and Defences to a perfect rare item without the effort of crafting or finding a rare item to buy.

Armour & Energy Shield For your rare Body Armour, Helmet, Boots and Gloves, aim for bases with Armour and Energy Shield to be able to use Armour and Energy Shield Mastery to greatly increase the defences gained from your Body Armour.

Eldritch Implicits You want to add Eldritch Implicit Modifiers using Lesser Eldritch Ichor and Lesser Eldritch Ember to your Body Armour, Helmet, Boots and Gloves once you have rare items that are good enough to not be replaced for a while.
Shield The shield is a critical item for the build.

You need it to provide most of your Block Chance against Attacks, but you also want it to grant some strong offensive stats such as Spell Levels, increased Damage and increased Critical Chance to improve the uptime of Elemental Overload , making this a tricky item to craft.
Campaign Low Budget Endgame During the campaign, try to get life and Resistances.
Acquire a Goat's Horn in Act 1 for its flat added damage After level 24 and locating the recipe near the Solaris Temple 2 waypoint, craft added Fire Damage to Spells on your weapon.
After the transition to Ignite Reap after Cruel Lab, try to find a Sceptre with +1 to Physical Spells, %increased Fire Damage or Damage over Time Multiplier.
Use Boots with Movement Speed and rotate between 2 Quicksilver Flasks for better mobility.

Reap Ignite Elementalist Minimum Budget Gear As you finish the campaign and begin mapping, your first milestone should be to have capped Elemental Resistances, positive Chaos Resistance and the correct Flask types, including a Bleed-removal and Freeze-removal suffix on the Life flasks.
To improve your damage while progressing the Atlas, aim for a 5-link Body Armour (or Vaal Caress as explained above), +1 Levels to Physical Spells on Amulet and Shield and the Obliteration Wand.
Upgrade into rares with high Armour and Energy Shield to bolster your defences for high tier maps.
Upgrade your flasks with well-rolled affixes and enchant them to automate their usage.
Reap Ignite Elementalist Low Budget Gear Your endgame shield with an "increased chance to Block" roll and a The Red Nightmare allow you to fully cap your chance to block.
Polaric Devastation (left Ring slot) and Death Rush are your endgame Ring choices for a large damage boost.
Your Amulet can have multiple strong damage modifiers, including extra Cold or Lightning Damage from the Shaper influence mod pool.
The Whispers of Doom endgame anoint allows you to use Elemental Weakness for an additional damage upgrade.
The Light of Meaning (Physical or Fire variant) in the Witch Jewel socket is another endgame damage upgrade.
Level 4 Empower Support is another way to upgrade DPS in endgame.
The Incandescent Heart is another endgame damage option, especially when it is corrupted with additional gem levels for socketed gems, duration gems or AoE gems.
Beware that with Incandescent Heart , you need capped Chaos Resistance, even when your Summon Chaos Golem is currently dead!

Otherwise, avoid the following modifiers: Monsters have 70% chance to Avoid Elemental Ailments Players have 30% less Armour · Players have 40% reduced Chance to Block Players cannot Regenerate Life, Mana or Energy Shield Players have 60% less Recovery Rate of Life and Energy Shield A n y t i p s f o r d e a l i n g w i t h p i n n a c l e b o s s e s ?
Single target damage is definitely the main weakness of the build.
Armour is not very useful against a lot of bosses, so swapping in Malevolence instead of Determination can make the fights easier.

Changelog Lightning Trap of Sparking Elementalist League Starter for Path of Exile Mirage 3.28 by Velyna Mirage 3.28 Last Updated: March 2, 2026 Follow Changelog FAQ Get Link Advertisement This website is supported by ads.
The Elementalist harnesses the power of elemental Ailments, Heralds, and Golems to provide a smooth and powerful league starter.
Shock enemies with Cold or Chill them with Fire , while gaining damage and defense passively through Golems that self-revive.
This means damage can be pre-stacked for bosses or thrown offscreen while mapping.
Trap throwing speed makes traps become armed faster, and the default 15 trap limit is increasable through support gems or passives.
During the Campaign and Early Mapping, you wear hybrid armour/energy shield rare items to provide Physical Mitigation without requiring too much Strength.
Before transitioning into the Low-Life setup for Endgame, ensure you have Shavronne's Wrappings and Presence of Chayula to protect against Chaos damage and Stuns.
Lightning Trap of Sparking Elementalist Gear Resistances You do not need specific resistances on any item, just get the required total resistances amongst all gear combined.

Skills Mirage 3.28 Lightning Trap of Sparking Elementalist League Starter Level 1 - 12 Lightning Trap of Sparking Rolling Magma is your main damage dealer until level 12, supported by Holy Flame Totem and Flame Wall .
Flame Wall is a lingering ground AoE which damages enemies and buffs projectiles you fire through it, useful for clearing packs or dropping on bosses.
Added Lightning Damage Support and Swift Assembly Support supports can be added immediately.
Use this behaviour to pre-stack damage for bosses or clear tough rare packs faster.
Shaper of Flames – Causes all damage to always ignite then partially converts ignited enemies Physical damage to Fire .
Fire damage is easier to mitigate because you already have 75% Fire resistance.
Shaper of Storms – Raises the minimum value of Shock from 10% to 25% while also allowing any damage to contribute to Shock .
Shock is a straight damage increase which normally requires critting or otherwise accessing Shock chance.
Mastermind of Discord – Causes Exposure you inflict to apply an extra -25% to enemy resistances.
This is particularly useful for bosses since they have some resistance by default.
Bastion of Elements – In the endgame Shaper of Storms loses value since you do enough damage to apply large Shocks without it.
Grab the important trap nodes like Expeditious Munitions and High Explosives , followed by Lightning damage.
Early Maps – After solving Resistances and Attributes, focus on scaling damage, block chance, and life .
Don’t overspend on the midrange Armour / Energy Shield pieces since they are only be worn while farming the gear for the Low Life swap.
Additionally, you must have a Shaper influenced shield with the “Recover #% of Energy Shield when you Block” mod.
It is recommended all rare items have some form of Energy Shield modifier such as “+# to maximum Energy Shield ” on jewellery or “#% increased Energy Shield ” on ES base armour /shields.
With these items gathered, switch to the Endgame Low Life tree and enjoy being able to run auras with your entire life pool plus 30% more damage from Pain Attunement .

Stat Priorities Mirage 3.28 Lightning Trap of Sparking Elementalist League Starter Learn more If you're struggling to survive, progress or want to learn more about damage and defenses in Path of Exile check out our Getting Started section.
Increased Lightning , Elemental, Projectile, Trap, and generic Damage.
Critical Strike Chance and Critical Strike Multiplier Exposure Effect #% increased Attack speed.

Endurance Charges + Immortal Call Energy Shield regeneration from Summon Stone Golem and Zealot's Oath 78/75 overcapped block Gain Energy Shield on block 40% of Physical Damage taken as Fire with Shaper of Flames Campaign Mid Game End Game Gearing Mirage 3.28 Lightning Trap of Sparking Elementalist League Starter Resistances You can get Resistances where v er you want and they do not have to be on specific items.
Explaining Low Life Low Life characters use Energy Shield as their Life pool, but do not allocate Chaos Inoculation .
This means Arrogance Support can be used to enable additional auras, and you have access to 30% more damage from Pain Attunement .
The downsides are not being immune to Chaos damage, which penetrates Energy Shield , and Stuns which are based on Life pool.

This means: You must have Chaos Resistance Your gear needs Life initially, but Energy Shield in the End Game Swapping to Low Life without all the required pieces can feel very bad Mid Game and Swapping to Low Life High Energy Shield rare items in Boot, Glove, and Helmet slots are important for End Game, so have them ready.
Mid Game chest is an Armour / Energy Shield rare, replaced by Shavronne's Wrappings for End Game.
Your belt and amulet remain flexible through Mid Game and can provide Life, Resistances, or Attributes while other gear is improved.
A Shaper influenced shield with “Recover #% of Energy Shield when you Block” is important for End Game because trap builds cannot leech.
Shields also supply Block chance and damage mods such as “#% increased Spell Critical Strike Chance” in the Mid Game.
Campaign Gear Early Map Gear Low Life Swap Gear Buying Goat's Horn wands from Nessa or picking them up from the floor is a very good boost to your early damage due to the flat damage implicit.
In the later Acts, be sure to have Arrogance Support leveling in the secondary weapon slots.

Lightning Trap of Sparking Elementalist Campaign Gear Armour / Energy Shield bases in the helmet, glove, boot, chest, and shield slots provide Physical mitigation before Enduring Composure is acquired.

Lightning Trap of Sparking Elementalist Early Map Gear Ensure you have all the following before swapping into the End Game tree and setup All Armour / Energy Shield bases must be changed to pure Energy Shield .
Shavronne's Wrappings is required to protect against Chaos damage by preventing it from penetrating your Energy Shield .
This highlights ALL the maps that you can manage, excluding bad modifiers for the build.

Otherwise, avoid the following modifiers: Monsters reflect 18% of Elemental Damage Players have (-12--9)% to all maximum Resistances Players cannot Regenerate Life, Mana or Energy Shield Monsters steal Power, Frenzy and Endurance charges on Hit Players have 60% reduced effect of Non-Curse Auras from Skills Players have 30% less Armour · Players have 40% reduced Chance to Block Can I play this in SSF?

Build Thought Spoiler Rupture support got buffed in 3.27, it can stack Rupture debuff up to 4 times now, so I wanted to make a bleed build that can utilize this support.
Why Bleed deal damage faster is good for this build?
Spoiler To fully ultilize Rupture debuff, we need a fast hitting skill that can apply rupture debuff as fast as possible, and also with bleed damage bonus, Bladestorm is exactly that.

The fast hitting of Bladestorm not only synergize well with Rupture support, it also maximize the bleed damage as we get to roll the damage 8 times, and only the highest bleed damage will be applied, effectively make our bleed damage 8 times more lukcy.

Because of how often this build can apply bleed, bleed duration is not something this build need, making bleed deal damage faster an effective more damage multiplier, so I stack multiple source of this modifiers on gears and passive tree, for example Usurper's Penance helmet.
Spoiler Since Rupture support require the bleeding hit to be critical strike as well to apply Rupture debuff, it is very obvious that it will go well together with Perfect Agony keystone.

https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Perfect_Agony Damage over Time Multiplier for Ailments is equal to Critical Strike Multiplier Critical Strikes do not deal extra Damage Non-Critical Strikes cannot inflict Ailments With this keystones, we can scale our bleed damage with Critical Strike Multiplier, the downside is Damage Over Time multiplier no longer has any effect on our bleed damage.
Spoiler Frenzy charge stacking is one of the best damage scaling for bleed build in POE, by using the infamous Olesya's Delight and Ralakesh's Impatient combo.
This combo allows the build to have both Affliction charge and Frenzy charge at the same time, a huge damage boost for any damaging ailment build.

https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Masterful_Form Your Maximum Endurance Charges is equal to your Maximum Frenzy Charges +1 to Maximum Frenzy Charges Slayer's Overwhelm ascendancy node also synergies well with the critical strike, Perfect agony that we are going with.
Elemental Ailments Immunity (Purity of Elements) 8.

| The Mechanics | The Damage | The Defences | The Countdown Heralds and minions are often chosen for their quality of life and automatic damage-qualities.
The defences The Arachnophobia Allstars are using a special combination of gear like the shield Aegis Aurora, Skin of the Lords and Melding of the Flesh.
Then high buffed global defences multiply your Es, your flasks, the aura discipline and triple in grace.
This way the long row layers of all defences is lined up: flat dr, armour, spell suppression, evasion, block and spell block.
The eastern deterministic uptimes of evasion, ghost dance and 100% spell suppression protect you in the nondeterministic downtimes of western blockings.
Infinitive recovery is overstacked with life-leech, life on hit, es on hit, es on block and es when hit.
This means that in combat all taken and dealed hits, spellhits, blocks and spellblocks heal you nonstop.
It's like drinking every second some lifeflasks and your blockings are never limited.

The Tree - Clusters & Jewels & Resis & Auras & Spectres stacking Because our biggest multiplier of HoA-dps is stacked 9-12 x on medium clusters "Pure Agony", we further collateral produce slots for 9-12 flexible inbuilt small clusters and jewels.

This architecture of our passive tree and a set-up of high-level spectres let us shine beyond in fast reaching the full 90% +max resis, 100% crit- and ailment-immunities and simultan stacking almost all tanky auras in really cheap budget.
The damage For all our damage we focus on our crawling immortal scorpion.

In higher skill-levels the herald of chaos is probably the highest damage-scaling minion in Wraeclast and is buffed into more than a billion dps by cluster-stacking the medium notable "Pure Agony" + beyond crits, curses, witherings, Unholy Might and in the Lycia-bloodline Chaotic Might.
Dozens skills can be utilized and transformed into triggering in high fullspeed the chaos-minion-projectile-damage of the ranged attacks of the scorpion.
In this build a dramatic cocktail of 3 different damagequalities is mixed and united.

With tempestshield, autotriggered tornadoes, void spheres and storm brands the herald is autobombing, with minion-poison 35M are balanced into continuous d.o.t., and the peak-damage is active channelling whirling cyclone to launch orbiting ball lightnings tickling the chaos-clone-beast-crawler's tail into fullspeed and initializing 100M to 8000M-fullscreen-chainexplosions.
The crawling dirty sting-king reaches millions more of damage than other herald-builds.
It shines with it's characteristics in full chaos-damage and unique 200-8000M dps in high content.

Tipps for Levelling in [3.27] Keepers of the Flame Spoiler While early levelling with HoA I always pushed a mix of different 4 sources of dps: 1) a mainskill like poisonous concoction with mandatory 80-100% chances to poison (guided in "HoA step by step", easy reached on the tree and with chance to poison-support), poison- / chaos-masteries, much thrown flasks (lmp, volley, attackspeed), of course fitting updated flasks and enough flask-charges which buff the damage too.
2) HoA - there are no keystones and not really damage-related passivetree-notables and no good buffing auras available.
A minion-offence-mastery is not optimal effective too to buff HoA-damage.
3) 3-4 sources of burstdamage: plaguebearer to melt bosses, vaal skeletons (or vaal absolution) against bosses, withering step for withers, and curses like despair, sniper's mark, punishment etc.
4) Then from lvl 54 onwards get fast cheap medium clusters pure agony and circle of nostalgia, they are the best damage-buffs at that level.
At that step Ghastly jewels, CIP or Arakaali's fang, Ebers Unification or support-spectrees are high damage also.
So for that gear a few chaos/alchemy-orbs are needed, around 3, and then for the first medium clusters around 3-10 more.
So HoA will be probably again not the optimal leaguestart-racer and choosing other skills until 5-10 chaos-orbs are gained is a good choice when you focus most on high leaguestart-speed.

New alternative gear Spoiler - Probably not recommended - A new helmet that gives headhunter to linked minions was introduced in the showcase: Link skills can target damageable minions Your linked minions take 73% less damage On killing a rare monster, a random linked minion gains its modifiers for 60 seconds That helmet would fit to buff the AG, Golem or the support-spectres.

___________________________________________________________________________ Spell Suppression nerf "Spell Suppression now prevents 40% of Damage from Suppressed Hits and Ailments they inflict (previously 50%)." The Allstars with their 90% flat dr, flat +max resis of melding of the flesh and their mix of defensive layers are very much less affected compared to all other builds with spell suppression as central major layer of defences.
When wanted put in 1-2 more rare jewels +2% max cold-resis or use a support-spectre for purity of ice to focus more into elemental tankyness.
Use 1 passive in a suppression-mastery for 4% increased suppression-effects.

Poisonous Concoction nerf Poisonous concoction now has 22-537 to 33-806 Added Chaos Damage at gem levels 1-20 (previously 30-716 to 44-1073), and Added Chaos Damage is equal to 4-16% of Flask's Recovery Amount if Charges were consumed from a Life Flask (previously 3-11%).
rolled flasks a 20% nerf to our recommended low-level skill and resulting now into less recommendations as levelling skill.

Against high-evasion bosses and mobs that's further equal to an around 10% more HoA-damage-buff.

Trickster "The Heartstopper Notable Ascendancy Passive Skill now grants Every 10 seconds, Take 50% less Damage from Hits for 5 seconds (previously 40%), and Take 50% less Damage over Time for 5 seconds (previously 40%).
The Soul Drinker Notable Ascendancy Passive Skill no longer has 20% increased Attack and Cast Speed while Leeching Energy Shield.
The Spellbreaker Notable Ascendancy Passive Skill no longer has +20% chance to Suppress Spell Damage, and now has Prevent +15% of Suppressed Spell Damage while on Full Energy Shield (previously 10%).

You can push this higher if you focus on armor itemization, Foulborn Ancestral Vision, and using armor flask bases.
50%+ chance to evade on stat sheet at the high end without using a Jade flask.
Ailment immunity through unique jewels or avoidance boot setups, though this feels hardly necessary once the character is up there i.
I use automate support for mine so it can eat incoming hits rather than limply trigger after taking a generally savage hit.
Depending on setup, further damage reduction from Flesh and Stone Offensively, the build has enough damage to do what it wants within reason.
War Banner dramatically ups your damage multi and gives you enough accuracy to turn on Precise Technique once your tree is more built out.
It essentially becomes a 3x damage multiplier button on my character at this point.
Dread Banner is the source of block and life gain on block.
Early on, this will cap your attack block, and as you gain more aura effectiveness and gem levels, you’ll eventually be able to cap spell block by taking Versatile Combatant later.
Defiance Banner is the source of suppression and flat spell damage reduction.

Between cursing on hit to free up a gem socket, upping valor generation a lot, and absolving me of the need to make a high DPS impact force propagator and roleplay as a Berserker, it's just well-aligned with the aims of the build.
For this, it's really nice - and it gives a good bit of damage too.
I have level 21 banners with a helmet corruption that pushes them into 11% more AoE per, and I use increased AoE support, which makes them usefully large.
The duration, after quality, incidental investment on the passive tree, and no other investment (such as more duration support), is 29.76 seconds.
War Banner (to ensure that Precise Technique is triggered) and Dread Banner (to ensure having spell block cap) are what are to be used as the benchmarks for if you have enough aura effect or not.
If you have level 23 banners, you need less aura effectiveness on the tree to hit spell block cap.

You can sometimes even give up your aura effectiveness body armor implicit and/or 10% non-curse effectiveness mastery on the tree like I have while still hitting 65% spell block, thus freeing up a few passive points as a stretch goal.
Earlier on, you might need every source to help ensure 65% spell block, including the 20% increased banner prefix.

Unfortunately, even in an endgame setup, due to the value of eldritch implicits, I think you'll always have 20% increased banner effect prefix on your body armor, even if it means giving up a reasonable chunk of armor/evasion.

I also recommend having some accuracy on gear if you can squeeze it in eventually to help make this a non-issue to let you run modest amounts -aura effectiveness mods while progressing through the atlas and doing non-T17 maps with lower aura effectivenss map debuffs.
There's also a possibility of hitting the next more % per valor breakpoint on each banner with a +4 corruption and an empower support, but I don't necessarily recommend it.
Using Empower Support also can be a good although sometimes unnecessary stepping stone to making sure you're able to cap your spell block chance and to gain more accuracy on War Banner.
However, ultimately, more aura effectiveness means more damage with War Banner, even after hitting block caps.
There is something to be said about diminishing returns here and if you think modifiers/passives are worth giving up over, say, more armor/evasion on body armor prefixes.
With enough warcries linked and CDR, we can have full uptime on The Wild Bear, granting stun immunity as well as some good damage/action speed buffs.

You could argue that you could also take Inspirational --> Worthy Causes --> Master of Metal --> Oshabi, but you will need to use Fortify as a support gem, you will need to be diligent, and you will lose max hit because of having fewer max fortify stacks.
Perforate of Duality hugely boss damage with extra button.
Earthshatter good general purpose, damage is inconsistent because positioning.
Prior to 3.28's regular Earthshatter nerf, it was actually just a few % off in terms of bossing single target, while having more reliable mapping damage - though you had to actually aim it.
It's snappy, has stupid high damage effectiveness in blood stance, has great AoE in sand stance.
For blood stance, since it's hitting with multiple spikes with solid damage effectiveness, I am leaning more on impale scaling.
More spikes from quality is more damage and more reliable damage at that.
Perforate in blood stance, including the transfigured version, has absolutely unrivaled single target damage.
For sand stance, its damage doesn't scale as much as Earthshatter or Earthshatter of Prominence, or even Ground Slam of Earthshaking for that since it has a *comparatively* low effectiveness.
However, the real serious selling point, and why I still use it for clearing is because its damage is almost instantaneous and it is omnidirectional.
Earthshatter is omnidirectional, but due to how spikes work, has inconsistent damage application/coverage, and the very fringes of the skill actually do less damage than sand stance Perforate.

The downside is that it strains my mana harder, especially with this league's 7L coins, so I have to use both mana cost passives and I find I need Charisma to have enough of a buffer to cast without OOMing.

Though I am talking Perforate up here, it is counterbalanced by its own mechanics: you MUST use a stance skill to use sand stance to clear with/ or use the skill at all really, which means you MUST reserve more mana.
I use both stance skills (35% combined reservation).
It DRAMATICALLY ramps single target by creating burst damage windows.
Plus, Ashes of the Stars takes a large bite out of Duality's less damage per spike on the gem itself, raising the ceiling of the skill even higher.

Earthshatter of PROMINENCE Spoiler Definitely worth considering - it in many cases does more damage after the 3.28 nerf to REGULAR Earthshatter, it's strong at clear, and it maintains its damage while bossing without any extra steps.

Earthshatter of PROMINENCE has extremely high damage effectiveness - I just didn't stick with it for 3.27 because I liked standing still in the middle of hive fortresses and having omnidirectional damage that was right next to me.
Example: Regular Earthshatter, post-3.28 nerf: 329 + (329 * 3 * .7) = 1020% damage effectiveness; base hit + 3 spikes overlap after the 30% tax.
Earthshatter of Prominence, untouched: 346 + (346 * 1 * 2 * 1.2) = 1176% effectiveness; base hit + the 100% more damage multi and 20% more damage multi on a single spike).

To quantify the value of Earthshatter being better at 1 hitting distant enemies, a 1 spike Earthshatter has 560 (or 329% if the first initial hit doesn't hit)% damage effectiveness if only 1 spike hits.

Prominence, on the other hand, has its full 1176% - twice the damage per hit, so hopefully you can see what I mean by Prominence being better at killing far things, being more consistent, but losing out on those glorious super concentrated shotguns of regular Earthshatter.
One of the main reasons why Prominence has strong damage is because it does not bear a tax baked into the gem that counters other scaling vectors.

For example, 30% less damage on spikes on regular Earthshatter means that those big Fist of War hits are eaten into substantially, whereas Prominence has a 100% multi that pushes those other things further.
Earthshatter's drawbacks: if an enemy is far, its damage per hit is less than sand stance Perforate or Ground Slam of Earthshaking.
This is a large part of why I adopted armor/evasion itemization so that I am less likely to be shotgunned.
This build is not crit immune unless you go out of your way to achieve it.
I have not yet determined some sort of elegant way of being crit immune without Brass Dome, which disables being able to benefit from hybrid itmeization.
Aura effectiveness does not break this build, but it can turn your Precise Technique off and lower your damage and general durability.
Aura effect with map effectiveness atlas nodes can hit your block pretty well.
Reflect is an issue, but can be doable with Mageblood (please do not kill yourself on reflect with a Covetous Shrine or Vulnerability amplifying your damage taken).
FAQs: What kind of damage are you seeing on the build?
Ryslatha's is also still the superior damage option.
Corruptions can impose even greater mana burdens in Mirage league, so they might be more attractive if coins do not go core.
-Maybe setups with grasping mail are possible due to the high resistance total of this build?
- Gravicius body armor for 35% banner effect with a high tier non-curse aura effect implicit is a lot of damage/accuracy (great for dealing with accuracy map mod).
- Uber Exarch gloves after picking up Mageblood, then swap out of permanent fortify in favor of Master of Metal without having to give up -10% reduced DoT mastery or slot fortify support.
This would bring me substantially more damage while still improving my durability a bit further.
It does however result in unbuffed resistances, chaos namely, being harder to solve for.
The easy way out is to use Mageblood and an Amethyst flask, but I might need to recraft some rares to rebalance stats to keep my 5th flask slot as more of a wildcard.

Duelist - Puncture of Shanking Bleed Gladiator - Perfect Agony Critical Bleed Build for all Content!
I eventually decided to pair it with the Saviour, since the Saviour also effectively doubles the damage of the weapon in the other hand, making up for the somewhat lackluster DPS on Varunastra.

We have the usual Slayer defenses of infinite leech, which is very strong when paired with the 20% instant leech from Weapon Master, endurance charges from masterful form, the aforementioned fortify, and a good amount of evasion.
For damage, I'm going with pretty standard phys crit/impale scaling, using an imbued cyclone with increased critical strikes to make up for the low base crit on the weapons.
Using the standard mana cost chest to make cyclone free in combination with an exarch implicit on the helmet.
- Dread Banner gives us block attack damage - War Banner gives more melee physical damage.
Crushing Fist is a Retaliation skill that you can use for a few seconds after blocking, whether it be an attack or spell that you've blocked.
It deals a large amount of damage in a Slam in front of your character.
This build uses this gem and scales its damage to astronomical heights, to the point of using it (directly or indirectly) to oneshot every single monster (Yes, this includes bosses).

Sporting a comfortable 82/81/80 maximum resistance at the time of writing, 5 endurance charges and 30000 Armor before flasks AND LUCKY 70% chance to block attacks / 67% chance to block spells, you'll be chilling in any map.
This means that when we Impale, the enemy will have 6 impales that each last for 8 hits, each storing 20.9% of our initial damage per hit.

This gives a total of 20.9% x 6 x 8 = 10.032x damage stored (Over 10 times the initial damage!) We also take the skill gem Lancing Steel We do not want to use this gem for the ability it grants on the tin, rather we want Call of Steel.
Call of Steel allows you to recall all impales on any target that is impaled, dealing Area damage around them equal to the damage stored.
In our case, this means dealing 10 times the damage of our first hit.

The ascendancy points focus on having a higher block chance, and making our block chance lucky, which roughly increases it to 90% chance to block, Retaliate skills, which make our Crushing Fist usable for longer, with a chance to not consume a cooldown, and the last point is an overall damage increase, which is useful for pinnacle bosses.
On the offchance that a single FIST did not teach your enemy not to mess with you, all it takes is Call of Steel to deal 10 times the original damage.
Fist of War (Second Most Important, doubles your damage) 3.
For your Auras, you will want Determination, a hefty boost to your Armour, and Pride, which will increase the physical damage taken by enemies, and later on enable your watcher's eye Jewel.

Finally, You will need 2 unique items: Facebreaker, which will multiply your unarmed damage by 7 to 9 times as long as you are not wielding a weapon, and Abyssus, which will grant you large amounts of damage and a large critical strike multiplier.
But Unarmed attacks cannot normally critically strike, so why mention it?
There is an annoint that makes every 4th Retaliate skill critically strike.
With these items, you will be critically striking for 3x damage every 4th hit.
Counting your FISTs properly will allow you to ensure maximum damage gets dished out to the tankiest of enemies.
Finally, you want physical damage with attacks in every single piece of gear you can find it in.
It adds physical damage to attacks on marked enemies, which is a very big damage boost coupled with the above multipliers.
For your flasks, Lion's Roar will help increase your defense by granting armour, as well as your offense by granting 10% more melee physical damage.

This build can still be upgraded further with items I do not currently have, such as a Flesh/Flame Jewel combo from the Champion Ascendancy, further improving the Impales of your character, Eldrich Implicits on your boots and Chest Piece, and better values for physical damage on jewelry.
As soon as you reach the first ascendancy point, you should take Determined Survivor to maximize your chance to block.

A: It is possible that your Cast When Damage Taken gem setup is eating all your mana.
The purpose of Cast When Damage taken is to cast Molten Shell automatically.
To this end, we leave both gems at level 1, since Cast When Damage taken cannot cast anything with a higher level requirement than itself (such as a level 20 Poacher's Mark).
If you level this gem all the way to 20, it will end up casting spells you do not want it to if they are linked to it, wasting all your mana.
If you hit a monster with Shield Charge or Cyclone and they get impaled, your Crushing Fist will not be able to Impale them for 5 seconds, losing out on precious damage.

We mostly scale physical damage (with axes) and not melee damage.

Why not eviscerate: Eviscerate has better damage than splitting steel and clears a big area, but only after you have been hit shortly before and in my opinion therefore its not very reliable for clear.
We use a 2h weapon (Sword or Axe with the most physical damage we can get) Before Merveil we get Lacerate as well, which helps with single-target.
Auras: Pride/Herald of Purity/Flesh and Stone (Haste if you have a good weapon and do not need damage, Purity of Elements for QOL if you prefer that).
3rd Lab: Block Chance After 3rd lab we switch to 1h axe+shield and get block chance on the passive tree as well.
Pick whatever 1h axe has the highest physical damage (gambling is decent to get one), if you want to craft reaver axe is the best base.

Early maps tips: For early mapping we basically need just life/resists and a decent physical axe (20% quality base and a couple shrieking/defeaning essence of contempt with the exalted increased physical damage craft is fine).
4th lab gives us lucky block, as soon as we get that, the build feels really tanky, so 4th lab is important.
Ryslathas coil gives us 30-40% more damage as well, so is highly recommended as well (if cheap).
If you reach ~5m dps with lacerate, maven invitations become a decent option.

https://pobb.in/OsndTNas1Ape Since GGG removed the old retaliation skills of Vengeance, Riposte, and Reckoning I have been looking for a way to make remake my favorite build of all time, Vengeance Slayer, with its replacement Crushing Fist.

In particular, this build relies on Foulborn Ylfeban's Trickery, a 2 chaos helmet that grants "Increases and Reductions to Spell Damage also apply to Attack Damage with Retaliation Skills at 200% value".

To best utilize this, the build stacks strength with the well-known Iron Fortress strength stacking shell to get upwards of 2500 strength, which translates to 750% increased spell damage and thus 1500% increased retaliation skill damage.

It also utilizes the Foulborn version of Iron Fortress to grant "+1% Chance to Block Spell Damage per 50 Strength", in conjunction with Tempest Shield this gives us spell block cap with essentially zero investment into spell block (the unlucky chance to block spell damage on Iron Fortress is then mitigated by the Runegraft of Stability, making it unexciting instead).
Like most strength stackers, this build utilizes Replica Alberon's Warpath to gain 1-80 Chaos Damage to Attacks per 80 Strength.

The massive damage range on these boots is generally mitigated by attacking very frequently such as with Rage Vortex of Berserking, or with a strike still that utilizes multistrike and many attacks per second.

Initially this problem was solved by utilizing another Foulborn unique, Foulborn The Red Nightmare, which in conjunction with a few tattoos and taking Diamond Skin in Marauder area grants 50% of fire damage converted to chaos.
Taking the Lycia bloodline and the Sinner Saint notable grants another 50% of all elemental damage converted to chaos.
We can then use Brutus' Lead Sprinkler to gain 4-7 fire (and thus chaos) damage to attacks per 10 strength, greatly improving both the maximum and minimum damage ranges.

Unfortunately, despite my initial plans retaliation skills are specifically unexertable (why, I cannot fathom), and so I eventually switched to Paradoxica which gives 100% double damage chance and allows this build to utilize two retaliation skills instead of one.
As a consequence, however, the damage can range from slapping the enemy with a wet noodle to dropping a nuke capable of phasing or outright killing an endgame boss instantly.

Note that the Paradoxica version of the build also allows the use of the non-foulborn Red Nightmare, which instead grants chance to block attack damage, allowing us to easily cap both spell and attack block with the investment of a single jewel socket and 3 passive nodes for the As the Mountain cluster.
Despite all this, the build is not actually reliant on blocking in order to deal damage, as is usually the downside with retaliation skills.

We can then utilize a forbidden jewel set to steal Measured Retaliation from Gladiator (a 20 chaos set, by the way) which gives us a 50% chance to not consume a cooldown charge on retaliation skills and makes them usable for another 2 seconds (which is then nearly doubled by Bitter Instinct itself).

In this version of the build, I use Second Wind support in the main damage link such that even if I were to get unlucky and hit the 30% chance to consume my cooldown, I would have an additional backup fist with which to continue throwing hands.
For damage links this build utilizes both Fist of War and Pulverize in order to maximize both area of effect and damage per fist.
Headsman and Bane of Legends then provide some much needed sources of more damage and cull.

This build has difficulty sustaining mana, however, and so we use lifetap on both our main link and several supporting skills which is suboptimal for damage but allows us to largely ignore mana as a concept.
If there was a simple source of mana leech you could use Close Combat support instead of Lifetap for an additional 20% more damage.
Defensively this build relies heavily upon the capped attack and spell block, as well as Slayer's overleech for recovery, then Stormshroud for elemental ailment immunity.
An impossible escape for Avatar of Fire allows for allocation of both Nomadic Teachings for +2% maximum elemental resistances and Crushing Reply which makes retaliation skills fortify on hit.
Furthermore, as this build utilizes entirely chaos damage, cannot miss, and does not benefit much from increased attack speed this leaves Determination as the premiere aura to use.
In this version the build has 50k armour and 4 endurance charges, providing decent physical mitigation.
A standard strength stacker using Crown of Eyes would certainly be able to out-DPS and out-recover it, especially for the sizable budget of this version.
Feel free to provide any comments, suggestions, or criticisms.
