Duelist - [3.27] Lightning Dancing Dervish, 7M, 130k ehp, help me solve ailment immunity please!
So i come up with this build for this league:https://pobb.in/7BbQTHgnwU3V I used to love this build and i figured out that i can cut the grace setup for the purity one.
The build is not minmaxed since i get too many resistances and i have nothing against dots and ailments.
Upgrades after that can cost from a few chaos to 10+ divines.
Basic Mechanics Spoiler Damage sources are Crushing Fist 6link and Earthshatter 4link impaling.
To scale damage and break faces we go facebreaker and get flat damage mainly from rings.
With Reckless Defense jewels and the Binds of Bloody Vengeance belt we can invest in crit multiplier too.
The defense relies basically on blocking everything .
You can achieve 75% regular attack and spell block.
From there you can get lucky block with Gladiator's ascendancy, and even roll the dice twice with Azadi's shield, reaching 98% block with 2 lucky rolls .
Showcase Spoiler Necessary Uniques Spoiler You'll need 2x Woespike (you'll use 3 rings with the new Nameless King ascendancy, and as many Reckless Defense jewels as you can have.
Rares Spoiler Use them initially to fix your resists with your tree, then upgrade as necessary.
I recommend starting with the body armour for the 6link and damage.
Before that you can use rare steel rings and a rare amulet to help with resists.

We are using 8 uniques , which means life and resists that come from rare items are missing, and you'll have to find them in other sources (like fire/cold resist tattoos , nodes from the talent tree , and cluster jewels passives).
For the shield slot you have two options: Azadi for defense and Great Old One' Ward for offense .
Upgrades Spoiler Large Cluster Jewels : search for nodes like Prodigious Defense, Veteran Defender, and Master the Fundamentals to fix your resists, spell block, and attributes.
The Champion's ascendancy Master of Metal can be obtained with Forbidden Flesh/Flame and provides a lot of flat damage with impales.
The Balbala variant also synergizes well with the new ascendancy for unlimited mana/life flasks.
The build does not have the 200M dps you see in PoB, but somewhere between 10 and 50M dps seems like a good estimate.
You do way more damage if you have a lot of frenzy charges but you're constantly going up and down the ladder, so you are rarely at max DPS.
If you have big balls you fix this by 3x grand spectrum frenzy and 6 portal defense.

It's also very easy to manage, a complete beginner-friendly build with only 3 skills to use; Leap, Lacerate, and War Banner.

PANTHEON: Major God: Lunaris Minor God: Ryslatha GEAR AND GEMS: 1) Required Gear(cheap, can do most content) and it’s Gem Setup (8.05m DPS, 840k EHP, 40-50 div budget excluding gems): Please read this one first before reading the next one(Meta Gear version) as I am telling all the basics about the gear we will be using here.
Spoiler On main hand, we need an axe that has 550+ physical dps and also having high attacks per second(at least 1.80) and then craft % increased Physical Damage from Runecrafting in Kingsmarch.
Cold Damage mod is not needed here and you also don't have to have Strength here.

We need Soul Taker on off-hand to be able to use Leap and Lacerate indefinitely thanks to "Insufficient mana doesn't prevent your melee attacks" mod, also to fill mana reserve with Pride, Malevolence and Herald of Purity.
We’re going to use a rare helm having life and then required resists.
Here we need to make sure we have armor/evasion as high as possible.
Since we use the Iron Reflexes passive node, evasion also counts as armor for us and evasion gear are more common and cheaper on trade.
To get the 7% increased Mana Reservation Efficiency of Skills implicit, we need to use Lesser Eldritch Ichors.
Don't forget to cap levels of your gems in your CWDT setup here like this: Cast While Damage Taken(lv3) - Immortal Call(lv5) - Vulnerability(lv7) - Lifetap(lv1).
We need a 6-link chest with as high armor/evasion as possible and run Lacerate on it.
We need Chance to Suppress Spell Damage, Life, required resistances, and %life/mana craft here.
We need gloves with Chance to Suppress Spell Damage, Physical Damage to Attacks, Attack Speed, and Life.
We need to use Grand Eldritch Embers until we get Physical Damage over Time Multiplier and Grand Eldritch Ichors until we get % chance to Suppress Spell Damage.
For boots, we need Chance to Suppress Spell Damage, required resists, life, and then craft Movement Speed and Onslaught on it.
Here if you cannot get a 20-20 Efficacy Support, lower its level to 16-19 matching the Intelligence you have.
We need a Stygian Vise with life, needed resists, and then craft %Increased Damage on it.
On our Abyss Jewel socketed in our belt we need physical damage to attacks, physical damage to axe attacks, and life.
For rings, we need physical damage and life on both rings, and then needed resists.
And then craft %Increased Damage on at least one of them.

We need a Watcher's Eye having increased Physical Damage while using Pride, The Light of Meaning with increased Physical Damage, Megalomaniac having Enduring Composure and Battle-Hardened, 2 large cluster jewels having these mods, one medium cluster jewel and 3 base jewels.

With each increasing level of CWDT, both damage we need to get for activation and gem levels it can support increases.
At level 1 it triggers when we get 528 or more damage, but it can only support gems with level 38 or lower requirement.
At level 10, it's 1354 damage and can support level 56 or lower gems.
At level 20, it's 3272 damage and can support lv 70 or lower gems.

When we increase CWDT level, frequency of skills supported will be cast gets lower(like getting 3272 damage happens less frequent than getting 528 damage), but it becomes able to support higher level gems.
At level 3, it's cast frequently enough, also be able to support Immortal Call at lv.5 and Vulnerability at lv.7.

Like when you hover your mouse on lv.3 CWDT, it says "This Gem can only Support Skill Gems requiring Level 42 or lower", and both lv.5 Immortal Call and lv.7 Vulnerability requires lv.42 so that's our limit for lv.3 CWDT.

(WIP) THIS BUILD IS A WIP, PLEASE GIVE CONSTRUCTIVE CRITICISM WHY I DECIDED TO MAKE THIS BUILD Alright so I decided to make a build based around Consecrated Path , and decided to choose duelist bc I haven't made a duelist build in a while.
The focus of that build mainly focused on this belt: The belt makes you take # damage whenever you use a movement skill, which would be perfect for triggering spells with a low level CWDT gem.

link here: > https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MWyV0kIp5n4 < The video explained how a Cast on Ignite build would work, using these two VERY IMPORTANT UNIQUES that I will be focusing on in this build: So how it works now is that I have a stable amount of self-harm being done so my spells can be frequently discharged, along with extra damage from Gluttony as long as I'm igniting enemies.
So now all I need is just a good amount of leech so I can sustain the damage I'm taking and a way to ignite enemies as often as possible.

It also gives an extra 2% of damage leeched against ignited enemies, an additional 10% chance to ignite , and a set amount of global damage increase while your character is ignited, which is seen on the next item Mokou's Embrace gives your character a 25% chance to ignite themselves , an additional #% amount to ignite, and 20% more attack speed / cast speed while your character is ignited.
It also gives a tiny bit of resistances for the safe measure.
The main point of having Berek's Pass is for the additional #% increased damage while ignited .
Some added bonuses include flat cold damage, additional scaled fire damage, and a bit of resistances.
The Razor of the Seventh Sun is an extremely important part of this build as it is the main source of leeching in this build and can solely sustain the health of your character.
With two of them, you convert 100% of physical damage to fire damage while using Consecrated Path.
The base damage of this weapon is moderate but with the added 100% multiplier against ignited enemies it proves efficient.
It also has an added 100% damage multiplier to burning damage if your character ignited recently, which isn't needed but is an added bonus.
The Gang's Momentum isn't a viable choice but rather a damage centered choice for boots.
However, it does have a good amount of fire resistance and helps increase your movement speed.

The boots give an additional #% to ignite and a high damage multiplier against ignited enemies Gluttony is a sidekick to the Eye of Innocence, adding an additional # of damage dealt to yourself whenever you use Consecrated Path.
Most importantly, it curses enemies with LVL 30 Poacher's Mark on hit , which gives 34 life and 15 mana gained on hit and an additional 35% chance to gain a frenzy charge on kill .
With enemies constantly cursed with Poacher's Mark, you almost always have the maximum amount of frenzy charges and a stable accumulation of mana.
Bronn's Lithe isn't a viable choice and like the Gang's Momentum, is more damaged based.
Consecrated Path gains 2 levels and has a high % damage multiplier .
They both have a flat added amount to maximum health and high resistances.
Try to get a "Consecrated Path deals 40% Increased Damage " helm enchant, as I haven't got it yet.
There's additional intelligence on the helm because without base stat increases on the other items you need intelligence to level up skill gems ( mostly Combustion Support ).
All skills, as long as they are supported by Combustion Support, have more than 100% chance to ignite.
Currently I use and Armageddon Brand One important thing to note about Vaal Molten Shell is that it should NOT be supported with CWDT as it has a separate use that doesn't concern CWDT.
Vaal Molten shell explodes whenever any physical damage is mitigated, which means whenever I take physical damage from Gluttony, Vaal Molten Shell explodes and deals a bit of extra damage.
Another setup that I have includes Glacial Cascade, Frostbolt, and Ice Nova, with to convert cold to fire and a cold to fire support gem.

I removed Berek's Pass with Pyre The recommended links would be Ice Nova = Glacial Cascade = Cold to Fire = CWDT I also have these support gems I use Haste instead of Anger because the more attacks per second I have, the more damage I take from Gluttony per second, which means more spells!

For Consecrated Path these are the support gems that I'd use, listed in the order of necessity: moar damage moar attack speed moar damage moar damage + moar chance to ignite moar damage END RESULT: Spoiler In game, your tooltip DPS should reach above 100,000 DPS after Consecrated Path and all its support gems are LVL 20.

Compared to last league the early numbers are much higher (dmg and defense max hits) and later on are only slightly better.
Molten Strike and how to calculate your damage Spoiler https://www.poewiki.net/wiki/Molten_Strike Skill by default converts 60% of Physical damage to Fire.
This build is Claw Crit based, triple element stacker with focus on Cold to enable freezing, corpse removal via shatters and Herald of Ice to enhance the clear speed.
Calculating your Damage In the Skills section of the PoB copy and paste Molten Strike gems, check "Include in Full Dps" and one part use for melee and other one for balls.
At the Calcs section of the PoB you will find this window "Projectile Count" is your base number of projectiles from basic MS attack.
Chain's of Emancipation is the go to for almost every non-Evasion melee build.
While at lower end of the damage belt will be annoying vs Invitations but that will become non-issue when your damage gets higher and you can burst down first Guardian and such.

There are some requirements the build imposes on the suffixes to function: *You need 100% ailment avoidance (boots and Ancestral Vision jewel) *You need to cap chaos resistance @ +70%, important for Eternal Damnation amulet *You need to cap elemental resistance *You will need one good Int mod 50+ *You need to cap spell suppression Accuracy can be on any of these helmet/gloves/ring and you'll need one suffix for Accuracy.
You don't have to have exact affix placement as in PoB or me.

Fixing Immunities: Curse, Chains of Emancipation Elemental Ailments, Boots and Jewel Bleeding and Corrupted Blood, Magic Flask Reflect, Awakened Elemental Damage support gem Before you get Unique flasks use Magic Ruby and Sapphire flasks.
Before you get Nimis, use Returning Projectiles support gem instead of Elemental Penetration support.
Ascendancy Spoiler Items Spoiler General advice IF you're going to craft armour items, go for the pure Evasion bases as they have 2x better odds for desired prefixes.
Evasion doesn't play defensive part in this build.
Dex bases are used just because they can roll Spell Suppression and because of the Evasion mastery that gives 15% SS.
Harvest will be most useful to fit and cap resistances properly.
Use it to switch one elemental resistance to another.
(Fire>Cold etc.) Prioritize investing into defenses first before damage.
Char will feel better and damage is very easy to scale later.

Unlock the following bench craft - Can have up to 3 crafted modifiers (Putrid cloister) - Prefixes cannot be changed (The Coward's Trial) - Suffixes cannot be changed (Oba's Cursed Trove) - Cannot roll attack mods (Caer Blaidd, Wolfpack's Den) - Attacks with this Weapon Penetrate % Elemental Resistances (veiled 1hand weapon) - % chance to deal Double Damage (veiled dagger/wand/weapon) 3.
The damage is very decent for the cost and it is easy to make.
Check in POB before you throw all your currency at it.
A T3 mod gives only 1% less damage than the utility claw on my current setup.
I like this version because it will fix the build nicely until you can craft really good gear, get all your levels and iron out every holes like resists, attributes, etc.
It is entirely up to you to decide if you want to use this or go pure damage but keep in mind that they both cost a lot more to make than the multi modded version.
https://pastebin.com/f56tLzGC Note that using this method you can accidentally hit T1 crit multi which is a very good outcome.
Bench craft double damage Body Armour Spoiler For every armour part it's important to be either pure Evasion or Evasion + AR/ES base because of the Evasion Mastery that gives 15% Spell Suppression.
If you're going to craft body armour go with base with T1 fractured Life.
1.Craft suffixes (Fossils/Essences), for as much resistances as possible.
If so, after you Scour craft again suffixes cannot be changed 4.Do t4 Aisling Unveil for Increased Maximum life + Increase Maximum Mana hybrid mod.
5.Craft Phys as Fire + Lightning hybrid mod Putting armour on 30% with Harvest enchant for Intelligence per %q will be required.
Amulet Spoiler Aim for the better Chaos resistance roll.
For Amulet to do it's job it's important that character is capped at +70% Chaos resistance.
If you're going to craft it to buy base with fractured chaos resistance as it's the mod with lowest Weight.
From there you can either: 1) spam Deafening Essence of Spite for any T2+ resistance and get Life with Eldritch crafting.
(*should aim preferably for 55 Int) 2) spam Scorched/Frigid/Metallic fossil until you hit T2 resistance and T1 Intelligence.
Gloves Spoiler Gloves with "added Fire damage against Burning enemies" are drop only gloves from Incursion.
Craft on bench "increased damage while Leeching" prefix.
For the implicits go with Gain 1 Rage on hit and spell suppression, if ss is needed to cap it.
Other useful mods are 5% Chance to deal Double Damage, Phys to Ele, % chance to gain Endurance charge on kill, 20% fire resistance (if you're missing on gear).
5% Chance to deal Double Damage although not mandatory for build to function is highly recommended.
Not because of the damage per se, but because that one hit that does double damage will proc higher Fortificaton/apply Shock/Freeze vs very tanky stuff.
Ancestral Vision This one will cap your elemental ailment avoidance.
Cluster jewels You want 8 point dagger and claw damage clusters.
Rare jewels Increased life + Crit multi with Elemental skills/fire skills/one handed melee weapons/global crit multi Double crit multi jewels are fine, you don't have to go for triple ones.
Also double multi jewels leave flexibility to fix either resistances or attributes with other suffix.
Watchers Eye I got one with Penetration + inc Attack Damage, because it was cheaper at the time and had good rolls.
Best one is with flat cold damage while affected by Hatred + inc Attack Damage with Precision.
Other ok mods are inc attack speed/crit multi while affected by Precision although give little less dps gain.

You need: -Life -Chaos res -Elemental res -Accuracy These links can be useful to find the ring https://www.pathofexile.com/trade/search/Affliction/zEvlKQ2U4 https://www.pathofexile.com/trade/search/Affliction/ZwJ87r0tQ Belt Spoiler Either going for corrupted or not prioritize Chaos resistance roll.
Flasks Spoiler Alongside of Unique flasks you will use Magic flasks with Alchemist's prefix.
Combination of these suffixes with particular flasks is prob the cheapest way to go.
I'd suggest that you don't use autoflasks but link them to one that you will press.
Just get any added elemental damage on it (fire/cold/lightning) while leveling.
Passive tree for leveling you will find in the Leveling PoB with different stages of progression.
You will need 2 mana flasks for Brutus so keep and eye for them.

Use Blood Stance -The Caged Brute Molten Strike - Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support - Life Tap (if you get 3L) buy Precision, level it max to lvl10, Whirling Blades and use them as main movement skill Claws while leveling, aim for ones with most of any type of damage (ele and phys combined) and attack speed, that's it Act 2 -Sharp and Cruel (spider) Molten Strike - Lesser Multiple Projectiles Support - Elemental Damage with Attacks Support -Intruders in Black get Blood Rage, Herald of Ice.
Buy remaining gems and level them in secodary slot (Berserk, Arctic Armour...) Proceed till maps while watching not to go with Life above MS accuracy!
Roughly when you start mapping or earlier if lucky, 5L body armor is needed for Crit Transition.
Ascendancy progression 1) Brutral Fervour 2) Bane of Legends 3) Headsman 4) Overwhelm To transition to Crit you will have to get 5L chest to add Crit Strikes support.

Similarly, the set of taunt nodes solves player and minion accuracy for us.
Master of Metal doesn't provide much power (although the 1k flat armour for us and minions is nice), but there isn't a great 4th option.
Note that the Ephemeral Garb on AG provides Crit Immunity, so this choice on watcher's eye is unnecessary.
Today, Brutality is providing way too much damage to ignore, but in the future, better/different support choices might make this a good warcry to sub in!

According to my calculations, the hard cap for attack speed is 4.7 aps for 0.6 sec duration (which is achievable with just 21/20 less duration support), beyond 4.7 aps you attack too fast for the spikes to have time to explode, which means that the closer you get to 4.7 aps, the more your dps increases in theory.

On top of all this, every time you attack, you create 6 cracks that create an exploding spike at their end, and these explosions can overlap, allowing you to triple your dps if you have three explosions hitting the target, and all this can be made even simpler by using concentrated effect support.
I don't know how expensive it will be, but in my opinion it won't be expensive for several reasons: a slayer would never want to use the block as a defense mechanism, it wouldn't make sense.

A gladiator would also have no interest in FF this node, knowing that the one before it is incredibly strong and that there are plenty of nodes from other ascendants (slayer in particular, which are 10x more interesting to FF for a glad), and finally, an average champion player wouldn't go for this node either, unless he had the same idea as me, but I think most will use classic defenses as determination.
So if I don't play determination, how do I manage defenses ?

Seriously, for physical damage, I will use 6 endurance charges + crab barrier + fortification + flesh and stone, and the physical damage that, by some incredible chance, might reach me won't do any damage at all.
According to PoB, I could tank the Uber Shaper slam without any problem.

For defenses against elemental damage, I cap my resistances, always use 6 endurance charges + flesh and stone and fortify, which is more than enough to mitigate any spell that would pass my 91% chance to block spell damage lol.

For dots, I am immune to bleed with Steelskin, immune to poison with the armor and evasion mastery, immune to corrupted blood with a jewel corruption, immune to ignite with a flask enchanted with "use when ignited," and immune to burning ground with the pantheon.
Because the build uses Dissolution of the Flesh, which is an incredible tool when paired with block and works really well with Petrified Blood.
Dissolution of the Flesh gives me a huge amount of life points that will help even more to avoid potential one-shots that would pass through the block.
So, lucky block + Dissolution of the Flesh + Petrified Blood + Flesh and Stone + fortify + crab barrier + capped res = tanky.
For offense, we use impale, crit and attack speed (mostly).
They got heavily buffed in crit chance and you can achieve stupid numbers like 11% crit chance on them.
So staff make crit cap really easy, but that's not all, staff also provide block support, and i'm play block as defense, so it's just perfect ?
Then we just use ryslathas to scale damage further, some attack speed here and there and we're done.

In fact all the rare gear in pob is self-craftable and easier than ever with the return of recombinator, even the 1000pdps staff isn't hard to craft with recombinator, so nothing is out of reach in the gear, really nothing.

And if the dps ever seemed low to you on the pob, note that it's just my guess about the number because pob can't do the math with explosion and overlap itself, so it might be higher or lower in game, and my guess is that is gonna be higher in game because pob doesn't calculate the impale dps with all the extra-hit we get from explosions.

Besides that, you want lots of Red/Green Sockets on Armour/Evasion gear that has good Resistances, and again, with as much Attack Speed and Physical Damage as you can find.
Finally, ALWAYS remember to keep your Accuracy Rating higher than Life, so you don't miss out on the damage boost from Precise Technique.

Spoiler https://pobb.in/WqyerfqZBzeQ I call it the Banner of War Champion, as a build archetype I will coin in this thread.
In addition, I utilize the Unique Body Armour, Perfidy, as the keystone unique for this build.

Main Features: Offensive Power with War Banner's More Melee Physical damage bonuses (and accuracy rating) Defensive Power with Defiance Banner's Spell Suppression bonuses and movement speed for kiting and map zooming.
Chance to Block boosts with Dread Banner, as well as additional recovery on block Pseudo-overcapped resistances within a banner's effect with the Rallying Icon notable.

Free 3 charges of Endurance and Frenzy when we place a banner with Maximum Valour Up to 63% Life recovered instantly with Recovery per Valour Banner Mastery Up to 10.5% Life regen per maxed out Banner with Champion's Inspirational node Mechanics: Valour - Lifeblood of banners.
Rallying Cry more damage per ally affected by the warcry 4.
Use Overexertion for Big damage: For my case, I have 4 warcries on me to get around 52% More damage with exerted attacks.
It's Defenses are inherently from the Health Regeneration, Free Suppression that the banners provide, but I also utilize additional mechanics: 1.
Precise Technique: With the Accuracy rating bonus from the Warbanner, you don't need to stack too much accuracy rating to achieve 40% more damage.
I also don't see myself investing too much on Criticals, I wanted to so that I can pivot with aggravated bleeds with critical/exerts, but I made this as a league-starter.
Echoes of Creation: With HP Regen inherent on this build, some PDR, I can actually take the 82% of my HP as damage each time I attack.
I also choose to get the 25% recoup from the leech mastery to mitigate it some more.
Echoes actually helped me with some mana problems as well when I take the 10% mana recoup from Mana Mastery.
Self-aura botting: I invested in two Reservation Mastery areas in my tree, and utilized up to 4 auras because Champion's Inspirational Ascendancy offers aura boosts.
Ancestral Vision: Since I go way above 100% spell suppression, I can get away with virtually free Elemental effect avoidance too.

By scaling duration, crit, and a little warcry cooldown reduction, This build can easily reach 10s and even up to ~60 million dps (~33 million shaper dps) without insanely expensive gear.
The best in slot weapon for dps is Marohi Erqui Unique hammer which has huge base physical damage a big attackspeed drawback which is mitigated by generals cry.

We are using hatred, hrimsorrow, and heatshiver despite the nerf since it is still the best method for scaling raw phsyical damage, of which we have a very large base thanks to Marohi Erqui which is hitting around 3 times per second with no attackspeed scaling thanks to generals cry.
Melee damage and pulverise support as well as the two hand mastery all give large extra damage at the cost of attack speed which are extra effective here.

If you go too hard on cooldown with generals cry it is possible to overdo the attackspeed reduction so if you want to stack all three damage for attackspeed reduction options you should keep our warcry cooldown to around 2.178 seconds which is the 84 total reduced warcry CDR from the two warcry nodes on the passive tree.
Alternatively, elemental damage with attack skills and the 40% damage against rare and unique enemies nodes are good alternatives if you don't want to spend passive points on attack speed.
Since we are using a two hand mace, Shockwave support allows your cyclone to help clear.
The primary damage from this entire setup comes from the new earthquake of amplification, with 20 quality the aftershock has a base duration of 3 seconds.
this represents 450% more damage (+300% from base gem).
With one of the duration nodes in the scion area, we get 45% increased and 10% more duration which brings it to 4.785 seconds which represents 705% more damage (+555% from base gem).

Using the Yoke of Suffering amulet with two of The Taming rings, we can reliable get triple elemental ailments with high shocks on enemies pushing our dps even further with some nice all res on the rings.
This is a decently high strength two hand build so The Magnate belt is perfect for maximizing your chance of double and triple damage for even more scaling.
The anoint allows us to grab clever thief for some sustain, or sovreignity to use determination instead of defiance banner and herald of ash if you have a life and mana steal primalist charm.
Frostblink also benefits from the stampede which we use to apply elemental weakness and bonechill for extra damage scaling.
For Uniques, either the Iron fortress or Spore Guard anointed with High Voltage for crit are the best options.
Sporeguard's damage reduction from fungal ground is particularily useful since it procs when you frostblink, granting an extra layer of damage mitigation.

The amulet anoint is Charisma to fit extra auras and we are running petrifed blood and low life at 55% maintaining an unreserved life slightly above low life so that Adrenaline can be activated on cooldown by using a life flask.
For the Slayer ascendancy, we pick Impact for AoE and accuracy, Crit and Culling strike.
Your main dps autotargets so you dont need to be in harms way other than applying curse and chill through frostblink.
The only downside is the 4.785 second delay on your primary DPS kicking in but once it does, everything melts.
Priority charm is %life and mana leech if you have killed recently with 30% chance to gain an endurance charge on stun.
Other charm priority is the crit chance/multi on shocked/burning enemies for offence and regen 20% life every 4 seconds for defense.
PoB: https://pobb.in/yPp5M1c1Bxx- Edit: updated PoB and gems with some optimiztion.
it is more safe Not really, shockwave on cyclone with Marohi deals plenty of damage for mapping and the earthquake delay kicks in right when you need it.

One change to the build is I swapped clever thief for sovereignty and dropped herald of ash and the banner for determination and blood rage because I have a 1% life and mana leech if you have killed recently charm.

If you want similar dps you need to go berserker but some of the main advantages of berserker (attackspeed from rage and berserk) do not help you reach the attackspeed/cooldown balance you need with generals cry, also rage gain doesn't work with your generals cry mirages, only your shockwave and cyclone will generate rage.
Those buffs still add damage to your mirages but not attack speed.
You can maintain decent rage from the warcry node though and the exerted damage buff is decent.
Berserk is still useful for the less damage taken but the movement speed is wasted since we are using The Stampede.
You will also need to find some extra accuracy which means on berserker you will need to get forb/flame undeniable fairly early which is a problem for low budget.
You can go Juggernaught if you want to be very tanky and get undeniable cheaper but it will hurt your total dps which makes soul eaters very scary no matter how tanky you are.

>Melee Damage on Full Life Support has been renamed Damage on Full Life Support.
At gem level 1 it now grants 30% more Damage while on Full Life for supported Attack skills.
It is notably no longer restricted to melee attacks or physical damage specifically.
Damage on Full Life Support now grants 1% increased Damage per 1% Quality (was 1% increased Melee Damage per 1% Quality).
>Melee Physical Damage Support now also grants 30% more Damage with Bleeding and Poison caused by Melee hits at gem level 1.
